title: Specialist, Content Optimization
skills: Experience with tools like Helium 10, Data Dive, or Jungle Scout.
Familiarity with Amazon's A+ Content and Brand Store modules.
Experience across multiple categories or markets.
description:
About the job

Brainlabs is the media agency built to answer one question: what's actually driving profit? Founded in 2012 by Daniel Gilbert, we were built by engineers before we were a media agency. Today, 1,000+ Brainlabbers across five continents use our proprietary agents, built on 32 media tools and over 2,500 logged experiments, to help brands connect every channel they plan and buy to one thing: the bottom line.

The role

Brands live and die by their product pages. A great product with poor content gets buried. A well-optimized listing with clear titles, strong keywords, and accurate attributes climbs. This role

owns that gap.

As a Content Optimization and Catalogue Management Specialist, you'll be responsible for how our clients products show up, get found, and convert on Amazon and Mercado Libre. That means writing and refining listings, managing catalogue health, and building the kind of organic visibility that doesn't disappear the moment ad spend drops.

You'll work directly with client teams and internal strategists to keep catalogues clean, content sharp, and rankings moving in the right direction.

What will you do?

Your day-to-day breaks into two connected areas.

Content Optimization

Content optimization is where you drive organic performance. You'll conduct keyword research, write and update product titles, bullet points, descriptions, and backend attributes. You'll audit existing content against platform best practices and identify gaps before they become ranking problems. You'll test copy variations and track what actually moves the needle.

Catalogue Management

Catalogue management is where you keep the operation running. You'll handle the publication and maintenance of product listings, manage bulk uploads and flat files, resolve listing errors

and suppressed ASINs or items, and keep content synchronized across multiple accounts and categories. When something breaks, you figure out why and fix it.

Beyond those two pillars, you'll monitor organic ranking performance, report on content health metrics, and bring recommendations that are grounded in data, not guesswork.

What are we looking for?

Experience

Around three years working in content optimization and/or catalogue management on Amazon and/or Mercado Libre. You understand how these platforms rank organic results and you've

influenced those rankings for real brands.

Platform Fluency

You know Seller Central and Vendor Central. You've worked with Mercado Libre item publication flows. Flat files, bulk uploads, listing errors, and suppressed items are familiar

territory, not new concepts.

Keyword and SEO Fundamentals

You know how to research, prioritize, and place keywords in a way that platforms reward. You understand the difference between what drives impressions and what drives conversions.

Analytical Mindset

You read performance data and draw conclusions from it. If organic rankings drop, you have a process for diagnosing why. If a content change works, you can explain what it did and why it helped.

Attention to Detail

Catalogue work is unforgiving. A wrong attribute, a missing image, a misformatted file: these things cause real problems. You catch errors before they ship.

English Proficiency

You're comfortable reading, writing, and communicating professionally in English. You'll work with international teams and documentation.

Nice to Have


Experience with tools like Helium 10, Data Dive, or Jungle Scout.
Familiarity with Amazon's A+ Content and Brand Store modules.
Experience across multiple categories or markets.



What happens next?

We know searching for a job is tough and that you want to find the best career and employer for you. We also want to ensure that this position is the best fit for both you and us. Therefore, you will participate in a comprehensive interview process that includes skills interviews with our team. The goal of this process is to allow you to get to know us as we learn more about you.

Brainlabs actively seeks and encourages applications from candidates with diverse backgrounds and identities. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace: we are committed to equal opportunity for all applicants and employees regardless of age, disability, sex, gender reassignment, sexual orientation, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion, or belief, and marriage and civil partnerships. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation during the application process, please let us know!

Please note that we will never ask you to transfer cash or make any other payment to us in order to apply for a role or to work for Brainlabs. Any such asks are fraudulent and should be reported to the appropriate authorities in your area.

Collaborate with peers across Capital One to ensure strategic alignment with enterprise social & blog standards

Basic Qualifications

description:
About the job

NBCUniversal is one of the world's leading media and entertainment companies. We create world-class content, which we distribute across our portfolio of film, television, and streaming, and bring to life through our global theme park destinations, consumer products, and experiences. We own and operate leading entertainment and news brands, including NBC, NBC News, NBC Sports, Telemundo, NBC Local Stations, Bravo, and Peacock, our premium ad-supported streaming service. We produce and distribute premier filmed entertainment and programming through our powerhouse film and television studios, including Universal Pictures, DreamWorks Animation, and Focus Features, and the four global television studios under the Universal Studio Group banner, and operate industry-leading theme parks and experiences around the world through Universal Destinations & Experiences, including Universal Orlando Resort, home to Universal Epic Universe, and Universal Studios Hollywood. NBCUniversal is a subsidiary of Comcast Corporation. Visit www.nbcuniversal.com for more information.



Our impact is rooted in improving the communities where our employees, customers, and audiences live and work. We have a rich tradition of giving back and ensuring our employees have the opportunity to serve their communities. We champion an inclusive culture and strive to attract and develop a talented workforce to create and deliver a wide range of content reflecting our world.





Job Description

Support the production and distribution of marketing materials (e.g., posters, banners), including vendor quotes and shipping logistics across LATAM. 
Maintain and update internal databases and spreadsheets (e.g., regional directories, documentation). 
Assist in the preparation of marketing presentations (e.g., highlight decks, big ideas, slate decks). 
Coordinate the ordering and distribution of promotional items. 
Upload and verify vendor information for the marketing team. 


Qualifications



Qualifications



Advanced English proficiency (spoken and written) is essential. 
Strong organizational skills with the ability to prioritize and manage multiple tasks under pressure. 
Excellent judgment and problem-solving abilities. 
Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and familiar with social media management tools (Mac and PC environments). 
High attention to detail and commitment to quality. 
Ability to meet deadlines while maintaining accuracy and professionalism. 
Self-starter with a willingness to learn and take initiative. 
Comfortable working independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. 
Open to feedback and continuous improvement. 
Strong interpersonal skills and ability to work with diverse teams and personalities. 

Desired Characteristics



A balance of analytical thinking and creative problem-solving. 
Intermediate proficiency in Excel (e.g., pivot tables, basic formulas). 
Curious, adaptable, and eager to grow within the marketing field. 
Passionate about entertainment, media, and storytelling.

Additional Information



As part of our selection process, external candidates may be required to attend an in-person interview with an NBCUniversal employee at one of our locations prior to a hiring decision. NBCUniversal's policy is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, gender identity or expression, age, national origin or ancestry, citizenship, disability, sexual orientation, marital status, pregnancy, veteran status, membership in the uniformed services, genetic information, or any other basis protected by applicable law.

description:
About the job

About Signify

Through bold discovery and cutting-edge innovation, we lead an industry that is vital for the future of our planet: lighting. Through our leadership in connected lighting and the Internet of Things, we're breaking new ground in data analytics, AI, and smart solutions for homes, offices, cities, and beyond.

At Signify, you can shape tomorrow by building on our incredible 125+ year legacy while working toward even bolder sustainability goals. Our culture of continuous learning, creativity, and commitment to diversity and inclusion empowers you to grow your skills and career.

Join us, and together, we’ll transform our industry, making a lasting difference for brighter lives and a better world.

More About The Role

We’re looking for an Intern to join our Consumer Channel team in Mexico.

Working for Signify means being creative and adaptive. Our culture of continuous learning and commitment to diversity and inclusion creates an environment that allows you to build your skills and career. Together, we’re transforming our industry.

As the world leader in lighting, we’re constantly ahead of the curve. Through our leadership in connected lighting and the Internet of Things, we’re breaking new grounds in data analytics, AI, and smart homes, offices, cities and more!

Signify is proud to be a leader in climate action, acting in line with the latest climate science. We are committed to reaching net zero greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions by 2040.

As an Intern / Apprentice you’ll be responsible for supporting Sales team and SOM role to improve customer service level in all the region (MEX & NOLA).

We’re on the lookout for forward-thinking innovators with a passion for sustainability. If you match this description, get in touch!

What You’ll Do


Sales Dashboard construction
Inventory level tracking (on hand / in transit) – Signify and Customer analysis
Slowmovers Action Tracker development
Sell In / Sell Out Consolidation ( 12 – 24 months)
Provisions report


Your Qualifications


Industrial Engineering or Business student (graduating after June 2027)
Intermediate English
Excel proficiency
Proactive


Our candidate profile is just a guide. We know that many talented people only apply for jobs when there is an exact match. If you don't fit all the criteria but believe this role is for you, we would very much like to hear from you.

We’re offering multiple opportunities for career development, like mentoring, coaching and stretch assignments, with strong international exposure for dynamic profiles. Learning is fundamental to our culture. Through continuous learning, we are transforming the industry. You have the chance to learn every day, acquire new skills and perspectives through customized online programs, and on-the-job experiences.

See #SignifyLife through the eyes of our employees!

Everything we’ll do for you

You can grow a lasting career here. We’ll encourage you, support you, and challenge you. We’ll help you learn and progress in a way that’s right for you, with coaching and mentoring along the way. We’ll listen to you too, because we see and value every one of our 27,000+ people. We believe that a diverse and inclusive workplace fosters creativity, innovation, and a full spectrum of bright ideas. With a global workforce present in 70+ countries, we are dedicated to creating an inclusive environment where every voice is heard and valued, helping us all achieve more together.

Come join us, and together we can light up the future.

title: Paid Social Senior Associate

Harness PMG’s proprietary technology, Alli, to streamline workflows, analyze performance data, and unlock deeper campaign insights, contributing to its ongoing enhancement.
Take ownership of project timelines and deliverables, ensuring campaigns meet strategic goals and exceed expectations.
Bring a proactive mindset and enthusiasm to each day, inspiring your team and making a meaningful impact on the brands you support.
3+ years of experience in paid social strategy or a related field, with a bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience.
Intermediate expertise in Paid Social and Content Marketing to craft, execute, and optimize impactful campaigns.
Foundational knowledge of Marketing Metrics and Marketing Planning & Analysis to drive data-informed strategies and recommendations.
Proficiency in social media platforms such as Meta, TikTok, Pinterest, Snap, and LinkedIn to set up, traffic, and manage campaigns.
Familiarity with tools like Google Analytics, Microsoft Excel, and SQL to analyze data and deliver clear, actionable performance insights.
Strong organizational and collaboration skills, enabling seamless alignment with internal teams and external stakeholders.
An analytical mindset, paired with a proactive approach to uncovering insights, testing strategies, and driving continuous improvements.

A commitment to curiosity and adaptability as a Continual Learner, staying at the forefront of media innovation while prioritizing client success with a Customer-Focused mindset that seeks opportunities to deliver meaningful value.
A dedication to People-Focused leadership, fostering collaboration, innovation, and professional growth by Leading by Example and nurturing strong relationships that empower others to excel.

Healthcare: Coverage and support for everyday medical expenses and routine care, tailored by geography.
Pet Insurance: Protect your pet's health and your finances.
Lifestyle Spending Accounts: Enjoy 100% company-funded accounts to promote healthy habits and well-being.
Commuter Benefits: Access support for travel and commuting needs, where available.
Annual Bonus: All employees are eligible for an annual bonus.
Volunteering Opportunities: Receive 8 give-back hours to volunteer in your local communities.
AI Enterprise License: Access AI Enterprise accounts and participate in weekly AI training sessions to empower and ensure AI safety.
description:
About the job

We are PMG

Named Ad Age Best Places to Work for 11 years in a row and Fast Company Best Workplaces for Innovators since 2022, PMG is a global independent marketing services and technology company that seeks to inspire people and brands that anything is possible. Driven by shared success, PMG leverages business strategy and transformation, creative, media, and insights—as well as its proprietary operating system, Alli—to deliver cutting-edge digital solutions and innovative media strategies.

Our team comprises over 900 employees globally, and our work for ambitious brands like Apple, Nike, Sephora, Experian, Therabody, Best Western Hotels & Resorts, Creed Fragrances, BNY, Intuit, CKE Restaurants, Travelex, and Whole Foods spans 85+ countries.

Professional proficiency in written and spoken English is required, including for resume submission and interviews.

Who You Will Be Working With

Join a team of innovative thinkers and collaborative problem-solvers who are passionate about the ever-evolving world of paid social media. Our Social team is dynamic, supportive, and dedicated to helping our clients achieve extraordinary results. You’ll work closely with strategists, creatives, data analysts, and technologists to craft campaigns that make a tangible impact on some of the most exciting brands in the industry.

We believe in fostering a culture where every team member feels valued, empowered, and inspired to bring their unique talents to the table. At PMG, your voice will be heard, your ideas embraced, and your growth supported every step of the way. If you’re looking for a role that blends cutting-edge innovation with meaningful collaboration, we’d love to welcome you to our team.

What You Will Do


Shape and execute paid social strategies that align with client objectives, overseeing budget allocation, media planning, reporting, and forecasting in partnership with cross-functional teams. 
Manage the setup and trafficking of campaigns across platforms like Meta, TikTok, Pinterest, Snap, and LinkedIn, ensuring best practices and precision in execution. 
Develop and implement testing frameworks to uncover actionable insights, translating complex data into clear, impactful recommendations for clients and internal stakeholders. 
Collaborate with PMG teams and platform partners to optimize campaign performance, offering real-time analysis and subject-matter expertise during client interactions. 
Harness PMG’s proprietary technology, Alli, to streamline workflows, analyze performance data, and unlock deeper campaign insights, contributing to its ongoing enhancement. 
Take ownership of project timelines and deliverables, ensuring campaigns meet strategic goals and exceed expectations. 
Bring a proactive mindset and enthusiasm to each day, inspiring your team and making a meaningful impact on the brands you support. 


What You Will Bring


3+ years of experience in paid social strategy or a related field, with a bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience. 
Intermediate expertise in Paid Social and Content Marketing to craft, execute, and optimize impactful campaigns. 
Foundational knowledge of Marketing Metrics and Marketing Planning & Analysis to drive data-informed strategies and recommendations. 
Proficiency in social media platforms such as Meta, TikTok, Pinterest, Snap, and LinkedIn to set up, traffic, and manage campaigns. 
Familiarity with tools like Google Analytics, Microsoft Excel, and SQL to analyze data and deliver clear, actionable performance insights. 
Strong organizational and collaboration skills, enabling seamless alignment with internal teams and external stakeholders. 
An analytical mindset, paired with a proactive approach to uncovering insights, testing strategies, and driving continuous improvements. 
A commitment to curiosity and adaptability as a Continual Learner, staying at the forefront of media innovation while prioritizing client success with a Customer-Focused mindset that seeks opportunities to deliver meaningful value. 
A dedication to People-Focused leadership, fostering collaboration, innovation, and professional growth by Leading by Example and nurturing strong relationships that empower others to excel. 


What We Offer


Professional Development: Take advantage of our learning and development programs, mentorship opportunities, and career advancement support. 
Generous Time Off: Enjoy generous paid time off and holiday allowances to recharge and spend time with loved ones. 
Parental Leave: We provide paid parental leave to support your family during important life events. 
Retirement & Pension Plans: Plan for your future with competitive retirement or pension programs, including contribution matching. 
Fertility and Family Support: Access fertility benefits for all team members and their spouses. 
Healthcare: Coverage and support for everyday medical expenses and routine care, tailored by geography. 
Pet Insurance: Protect your pet's health and your finances. 
Lifestyle Spending Accounts: Enjoy 100% company-funded accounts to promote healthy habits and well-being. 
Commuter Benefits: Access support for travel and commuting needs, where available. 
Annual Bonus: All employees are eligible for an annual bonus. 
Volunteering Opportunities: Receive 8 give-back hours to volunteer in your local communities. 
AI Enterprise License: Access AI Enterprise accounts and participate in weekly AI training sessions to empower and ensure AI safety. 


Benefits vary by country, location, and geography to reflect local laws, norms, and expectations.

What Sets Us Apart

Being part of PMG means joining a company culture that’s unmatched in digital. We're dedicated to working hard to serve our employees and clients, delivering value, results, and innovation—which often requires true grit and agility. We believe in taking care of ourselves and each other to continuously improve in every way.

In alignment with our core values to be inclusive and always change for the better, PMG is committed to creating a more diverse and inclusive culture, and we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We believe that we only change for the better by bringing diverse perspectives to our company. PMG recruits, employs, trains, compensates, and promotes regardless of race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status.

title: Digital Marketing Analyst
description:
About the job

It's fun to work in a company where people truly believe in what they are doing!

NEOGEN Digital Marketing Analyst support our LATAM digital marketing team. The responsibilities will include social media management for our LATAM accounts, it is Facebook, LinkedIn, and Instagram; execute campaign strategies and create marketing digital assets as email templates, forms, and landing pages on Eloqua (marketing automation), SEO, website optimization and maintenance. This role will also be responsible for assign new leads to sales representant on Salesforce CRM; collect and analyze data from social media, google and Eloqua for digital reports.

The Marketing Automation Specialist is responsible for creating and developing content for forms, landing pages, and email templates within Eloqua, supporting website design, development, and planning, and executing marketing campaigns across multiple digital channels. This role manages lead generation and lead routing processes, supports content ecosystem administration through platforms such as Bynder and Seismic, and monitors marketing KPIs and campaign performance to optimize results.

The position requires maintaining consistency and compliance with brand guidelines, messaging, and approved materials, using only content validated by marketing, technical, and legal stakeholders to ensure traceability and compliance. The incumbent is also responsible for keeping Quality Management System (QMS) documentation up to date, contributing to preventive actions and continuous process improvements, and maintaining an organized and compliant work environment.

In alignment with Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) standards, the employee is expected to use all applicable personal and collective protective equipment, maintain an appropriate workstation, take active breaks, and manage workloads to support personal health and performance.

The successful candidate demonstrates and embodies Neogen's Pillars of Trust—Openness, Honesty, Credibility, Respect, and Service—through all actions, behaviors, and communications. They communicate effectively, actively listen, lead by example, foster teamwork and trust, make timely decisions, solve problems efficiently, and drive performance through accountability, productivity, and innovation. They also understand their role within the broader organization and remain curious about how their work contributes to business success.

Qualifications include a college degree, preferably a Bachelor's degree in Marketing, with at least one year of relevant experience (two years preferred). Candidates should possess knowledge of marketing principles, Microsoft Office applications, CRM systems, and marketing automation platforms, with experience in Eloqua and Salesforce preferred. Additional expertise in digital marketing, Google Ads, Meta Ads, LinkedIn Ads, Google Analytics, UX/UI design, SEO, SEM, marketing analytics, business intelligence tools, and reporting is highly desirable. An intermediate to advanced level of English is required.

Come Be Part Of A Mission that Matters!

From inside the farm gate to our dinner plates, Neogen protects the world’s food supply. Through a variety of animal healthcare products, to food safety solutions for dangerous bacteria, allergens, toxins, drug residues and much more, Neogen is there — and you can be too.

About the job

📽️ WATCH THIS QUICK LOOM VIDEO ON HOW TO GET HIRED: https://www.loom.com/share/345f776084e642ba99c24aabe40a67a0

🇬🇧 C1 ENGLISH LEVEL IS REQUIRED. THE SELECTED CANDIDATE WILL BE SPEAKING STRICTLY IN ENGLISH DURING THEIR ENTIRE SHIFT.

🎤 PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND AUDIO RECORDING IN ENGLISH.




🚀 JOB TITLE: Marketing & Content Creation Specialist

🆔 JOB ID: KAYKYL1R

🏭 INDUSTRY: Marketing / Content Creation

📍 LOCATION: LATAM / PH / South Africa / Egypt

💼 JOB STATUS: Full Time

🕒 WORK SCHEDULE: Flexible Schedule with Mandatory Overlap 1–2 Times per Week for Strategy Calls

💵 SALARY: $8 – $10 USD per hour

📅 TARGET START DATE: ASAP

⚠️ PLEASE NOTE: This role requires a highly independent and creative content professional who can transform raw content into polished, publish-ready media with minimal supervision. We are looking for someone who thrives in both creative storytelling and process-driven execution while maintaining exceptional attention to detail and confidentiality.

ROLE OVERVIEW

About the Client:




Our client is a U.S.-based organization that provides physical, occupational, and speech therapy services, primarily serving senior living and retirement communities. Alongside their healthcare business, the founders also operate a personal creator brand focused on family life, outdoor adventures, and long-form storytelling through digital media platforms.

This position supports both sides of the business, each with distinct audiences, objectives, and content requirements. The ideal candidate must be able to adapt communication styles, creative approaches, and execution strategies accordingly.

About the Role:




We are seeking a full-time Marketing & Content Creation Specialist with strong video editing, content management, and social media execution experience.

This role is ideal for someone who can independently manage deliverables, organize content workflows, and transform provided footage, images, and written materials into engaging, high-quality content without constant oversight.

This is not an on-camera role and does not involve clinical or administrative responsibilities. Instead, your primary focus will be content production, platform management, organization, and performance tracking.




Two Distinct Content Streams Critical to This Role

Creative and Long Form Content Higher Effort

This portion of the role is more time consuming and highly creative.

Responsibilities include:

Editing long form YouTube videos ranging from ten to twenty minutes using raw family style footage such as outdoor activities, trips, and daily life moments.
Combining multiple video clips, photos, and audio into cohesive and engaging stories.
Making creative decisions related to clip selection, pacing, music using royalty free sources only, transitions, and overall story flow.
Repurposing long form videos into short form content for platforms such as YouTube Shorts, TikTok, and Instagram Reels.
Understanding that YouTube's growth strategy differs significantly from TikTok and Instagram, and adjusting editing style and structure accordingly.
Professional and Compliance Focused Content Lower Creativity Higher Precision

This portion of the role is more structured and process driven.

Responsibilities include:

Light editing and formatting of healthcare related videos such as product demonstrations and therapy tools.
Managing posting and scheduling across Facebook, TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and creator or affiliate platforms.
Publishing clinician spotlights, company updates, educational posts, clinic events, therapy activities, and patient success stories.
Creating or refining simple educational graphics and infographics using provided content.
Ensuring a professional tone, brand consistency, patient privacy, and compliance with healthcare data protection standards.

Most of this content will be provided or clearly outlined. The focus of the role is execution, refinement, accuracy, and consistency.

Key Responsibilities

1. Content Production & Editing

Video Editing: Execute high-quality edits for both long-form (YouTube/Webinars) and short-form (Reels/TikTok/Shorts) content.
Optimization: Apply platform-specific formatting, captions, and technical SEO to maximize reach.

2. Distribution & Management

Social Media Operations: Manage the end-to-end posting process, including scheduling and caption implementation across all platforms.
Digital Asset Management: Maintain a highly organized filing system via Google Drive and shared folders for easy retrieval.

3. Analytics & Reporting

Performance Tracking: Monitor and report on core KPIs, including views, engagement rates, and follower growth.

Role Scope & Boundaries (Non-Negotiable)

Operational Status: This is an Execution-Only role. The Editor acts as the technical hands of the brand, not the clinical or strategic voice.

Core Expectations

Confidentiality: Maintaining the "Fort Knox" integrity of sensitive healthcare and family data.
Accuracy: Ensuring provided compliance guidance is followed to the letter during the editing process.
Efficiency: Streamlining the workflow from raw footage to scheduled post.

Qualifications & Skills

Core Competencies (Must-Have)

Video Editing Mastery: Expert-level proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro, CapCut, or DaVinci Resolve. You must be capable of handling complex long-form YouTube edits (storytelling, pacing, audio leveling) in addition to high-energy short-form clips.
Platform IQ: A deep understanding of the technical differences between YouTube, TikTok, and Instagram, specifically regarding audience retention and platform-specific algorithms.
Precision & Context: High-level English comprehension to ensure captions are 100% accurate and medical/professional context is preserved.
Operational Discipline:
Strong self-management skills with the ability to meet deadlines under minimal supervision.
Willingness to utilize Hubstaff for transparent time-tracking and productivity.










Experience & "Bonus" Assets (Preferred)

U.S. Market Experience: Prior experience working with U.S.-based clients or creators.
Niche Familiarity: Background in Healthcare, Wellness, or Professional Services (understanding the tone and "feel" of high-compliance industries).

Affiliate Ecosystems: Familiarity with Amazon Creator or other affiliate marketing platforms is a significant plus.

WHY JOIN?

Creative Variety:

Work across both a professional healthcare organization and an engaging lifestyle creator brand

High Ownership Role:

Take ownership of content production and become a trusted extension of the business

Long-Term Opportunity:

Join a growing organization seeking a reliable long-term team member

Flexible Work Environment:

Enjoy a flexible schedule while collaborating directly with founders and decision-makers

✨ Join a growing network of exceptional remote professionals

📩 Submit your application today and take the next step in your career!

About the job

Paid Ads Specialist (CPG Brands)

Part-Time | Remote | US Business Hours




About the Company




Our client is a fast-growing US-based CPG food brand known for its bold flavors, premium ingredients, and strong brand identity. The company has built a loyal customer base through a combination of high-quality products, creative marketing, and a focus on delivering exceptional consumer experiences.

They are looking for an experienced Paid Ads Specialist to lead and optimize paid media efforts across Meta and Google, driving customer acquisition, revenue growth, and profitable scaling.




About the Role

This role is ideal for a strategic and hands-on paid media professional with deep experience managing performance marketing campaigns for consumer packaged goods (CPG), food & beverage, or eCommerce brands.

You will own campaign strategy, execution, testing, optimization, and reporting across paid acquisition channels, working closely with leadership to scale revenue while maintaining strong efficiency metrics.




Key Responsibilities

Manage and optimize paid advertising campaigns across Meta and Google Ads

Develop customer acquisition strategies focused on profitable growth

Monitor and improve key performance metrics including ROAS, CAC, CPA, CTR, and conversion rate

Build and execute testing frameworks for creatives, audiences, landing pages, and offers

Analyze campaign performance and identify scaling opportunities

Manage advertising budgets and allocation across channels

Create performance reports and present insights and recommendations

Collaborate with creative and marketing teams to improve campaign results

Support seasonal promotions, product launches, and revenue-driving initiatives

Stay current on platform updates, industry trends, and best practices




Requirements

6+ years of hands-on experience managing Meta and/or Google Ads campaigns
Strong experience working with CPG, food & beverage, consumer brands, or eCommerce companies
Proven track record scaling paid media campaigns while maintaining profitability
Deep understanding of ROAS, CAC, CPA, MER, CTR, and customer acquisition economics
Experience managing significant monthly advertising budgets

Experience scaling seasonal and promotional campaigns

Descripción Empresa

El Puerto de Liverpool es la empresa líder en tiendas departamentales en México. Contamos con cinco unidades de negocio: Tiendas Liverpool, Tiendas Suburbia, Centros Comerciales Galerías, Boutiques y Negocios Financieros, componiendo una empresa sólida, que ofrece a sus colaboradores estabilidad financiera y el orgullo de pertenecer a una EMPRESA 100% MEXICANA.

NUESTRA MISIÓN ES SERVIR AL CLIENTE EN TODO LUGAR, TODOS LOS DÍAS, TODA LA VIDA, nuestra visión es ser la mejor opción en servicio, surtido y valor. Nuestros valores nos guían hacia el trabajo en equipo, productividad, innovación e integridad.

Misión Del Cargo

Apoyar y liderar la estrategia de Brand Marketing de e-commerce junto a la Gerencia, asegurando planeación, ejecución y seguimiento.

Funciones Del Cargo


 Apoyar la ejecución de iniciativas de Brand Marketing e-commerce en todas las etapas del funnel (awareness, consideración y conversión), asegurando alineación entre mensajes, audiencias y canales según la estrategia y objetivos comerciales de cada marca y categoría, para garantizar su posicionamiento en el mercado.
Crear y colaborar en la adaptación local de campañas globales de marketing y publicidad, ajustadas a las necesidades de clientes y canales tradicionales o digitales, dando seguimiento a entregables creativos e implementación para fortalecer alcance, visibilidad y posicionamiento de la marca.
Gestionar campañas de temporada, lanzamientos y colaboraciones, coordinando planes de marketing (GTM), briefs y agencias creativas, medios, PR e influencers, con objetivos claros por etapa del funnel y siempre dentro del presupuesto de inversión (A&P budget).
Colaborar con equipos de Wholesale, Retail, Digital, Trade, VM y PR para asegurar que las campañas se adapten a los distintos canales y necesidades de la marca, garantizando posicionamiento adecuado y apego al presupuesto de inversión.
Analizar, dar seguimiento y gestionar administrativamente el desempeño de marketing mediante KPI’s, reportes post-campaña y control de presupuesto, asegurando procesos eficientes con agencias y proveedores, cumplimiento de tiempos y entregables definidos en los planes de marketing (GTM).


Requisitos


Lic. Marketing, Publicidad Comunicación, Administración o afín
Inglés B2-C1 INDISPENSABLE (se aplica prueba de speaking en tiempo real)
Manejo de SAP básico: ordenes de compra, ppto, autorización de órdenes de compra
Excel FUNCIONES: Manejo de base de datos, para llevar el ppto, formulas, tablas dinámicas
Shooting de producto INTERMEDIO
Canva o Prezi para Presentació de resultados, ppto, data dura de crecimiento, gastos etc
1.5 a 3 años de Experiencia en: ECOMMERCE, SOCIAL MEDIA
Conocimiento profundo del mercado de moda en México (fast fashion, lifestyle, sportswear)
Dominio de medios tradicionales y digitales para creación y adaptación de campañas


Beneficios

Condiciones oferta:


 Seguro médico
 Capacitaciones y cursos
 Seguro médico
 Descuentos en productos
 Vales de despensa
 Horario flexible
 Día de cumpleaños
 Vacaciones extras


Descripción Proceso De Selección

Para participar necesitas:


 Postular a la oferta en el link
 Revisar tu mail y confirmar tu cuenta
 Ingresar a AIRA y contestar las preguntas y/o pruebas solicitadas


Si tu perfil se ajusta a lo que estamos buscando, te contactaremos para continuar con el proceso.

description:
About the job

Descripción Empresa

El Puerto de Liverpool es la empresa líder en tiendas departamentales en México. CON MÁS DE 170 AÑOS EN EL MERCADO, nos dedicamos a facilitar la selección de productos y brindarles el mejor servicio a las familias mexicanas, esperando superar las expectativas de calidad, moda, valor y atención dentro de un ambiente agradable.

Contamos con cinco unidades de negocio: Tiendas Liverpool, Tiendas Suburbia, Centros Comerciales Galerías, Boutiques y Negocios Financieros, componiendo una empresa sólida, que ofrece a sus colaboradores estabilidad financiera y el orgullo de pertenecer a una EMPRESA 100% MEXICANA.

NUESTRA MISIÓN ES SERVIR AL CLIENTE EN TODO LUGAR, TODOS LOS DÍAS, TODA LA VIDA, nuestra visión es ser la mejor opción en servicio, surtido y valor. Nuestros valores nos guían hacia el trabajo en equipo, productividad, innovación e integridad.

title: Ejecutivo de medios offline
skills: Licenciatura en Mercadotecnia, Publicidad, Comunicación, Administración o afines.
Experiencia mínima de 2 años en implementación, ejecución y seguimiento de campañas offline.
Experiencia indispensable en agencias de medios o centrales de medios.
Conocimiento de medios tradicionales como TV, Radio, OOH, Prensa y Revistas.
Inglés intermedio.
Manejo de Excel y herramientas de seguimiento y control de campañas.
Apoyar en la elaboración de planes tácticos mediante análisis de audiencias, competencia y tendencias de mercado.
Ejecutar la compra de medios tradicionales (TV, Radio, OOH, Prensa y Revistas) de acuerdo con los planes aprobados por el cliente.
Gestionar la generación y seguimiento de órdenes de compra en los sistemas internos de la compañía.
Coordinar el envío de materiales creativos y dar seguimiento con medios y proveedores para la correcta implementación de campañas.
Solicitar, recopilar y validar testigos para asegurar la correcta transmisión o publicación de las campañas.
Monitorear campañas activas y dar seguimiento a incidencias durante su ejecución.
Realizar conciliaciones de campañas verificando que la ejecución coincida con lo planeado y contratado.
Gestionar el cierre administrativo de campañas y órdenes de compra para el proceso de facturación.
Elaborar reportes post-buy y análisis de resultados de campaña.
Un entorno laboral positivo y colaborativo, basado en el respeto y el trabajo en equipo.
Excelentes beneficios que promueven tu bienestar personal y tu desarrollo profesional.
Prestaciones superiores a las de la ley.
Un espacio seguro y libre de discriminación, donde cada voz es valorada y cada persona realmente importa.
description:
About the job

Acerca de nosotros




Somos una agencia global, especialista en medios, marketing y tecnología. Líderes en el ramo con presencia en más de 100 países. Creamos soluciones integrales para todos nuestros clientes de forma rápida y eficaz.




Nuestra búsqueda constante de mejorar en lo que hacemos comienza contactando con personas como tú.




OMG te invita a unirte a nuestro equipo como: Ejecutivo de medios offline




¿Qué buscamos?




Buscamos un perfil con experiencia en la ejecución y seguimiento de campañas de medios tradicionales dentro de agencias de medios o centrales de medios, con interés en desarrollarse en un entorno dinámico y colaborativo.




Licenciatura en Mercadotecnia, Publicidad, Comunicación, Administración o afines.
Experiencia mínima de 2 años en implementación, ejecución y seguimiento de campañas offline.
Experiencia indispensable en agencias de medios o centrales de medios.
Conocimiento de medios tradicionales como TV, Radio, OOH, Prensa y Revistas.
Inglés intermedio.
Manejo de Excel y herramientas de seguimiento y control de campañas.




¿Cuáles son las principales actividades?




Apoyar en la elaboración de planes tácticos mediante análisis de audiencias, competencia y tendencias de mercado.
Ejecutar la compra de medios tradicionales (TV, Radio, OOH, Prensa y Revistas) de acuerdo con los planes aprobados por el cliente.
Gestionar la generación y seguimiento de órdenes de compra en los sistemas internos de la compañía.
Coordinar el envío de materiales creativos y dar seguimiento con medios y proveedores para la correcta implementación de campañas.
Solicitar, recopilar y validar testigos para asegurar la correcta transmisión o publicación de las campañas.
Monitorear campañas activas y dar seguimiento a incidencias durante su ejecución.
Realizar conciliaciones de campañas verificando que la ejecución coincida con lo planeado y contratado.
Gestionar el cierre administrativo de campañas y órdenes de compra para el proceso de facturación.
Elaborar reportes post-buy y análisis de resultados de campaña.




¿Qué ofrecemos?




Un entorno laboral positivo y colaborativo, basado en el respeto y el trabajo en equipo.
Excelentes beneficios que promueven tu bienestar personal y tu desarrollo profesional.
Prestaciones superiores a las de la ley.
Un espacio seguro y libre de discriminación, donde cada voz es valorada y cada persona realmente importa.




Si estás buscando más que solo un empleo y quieres formar parte de una comunidad que te inspira y te impulsa a alcanzar tu máximo potencial, ¡nos encantaría conocerte!




En OMG NO discriminamos por razón de raza, origen étnico, edad, sexo, género, estado civil, ideología, opiniones políticas, nacionalidad, religión, orientación sexual, identidad o expresión de género o cualquier otra condición personal, física o social. A través de ello, construimos una cultura incluyente, de respeto y diversa.

title: Marketing Data Scientist, Data & AI Enablement
skills: Collaborate closely with the Snowflake engineering team to define, spec, and validate data requirements for models and AI applications
Serve as the translation layer between data engineering and AI/ML teams, ensuring data is structured, clean, and optimized for downstream use
Enable and support agentic AI workflows by designing and maintaining the data layers that power agent memory, context retrieval, and tool-use
Build and maintain data models and transformation logic on top of Snowflake to support predictive and generative AI use cases
Identify and resolve data quality issues in collaboration with the engineering team
Partner with marketing science and analytics teams to ensure data assets are accessible and model-ready
Automate and streamline data validation processes to improve reliability of AI-facing data pipelines
Deep fluency in Snowflake - comfortable living in it daily as a power user, and confident enough to hold technical conversations with Snowflake engineers
A strong understanding of how data must be structured and governed to reliably power AI and machine learning systems
The ability to translate business and modeling needs into precise, actionable data requirements for engineering teams
A collaborative mindset with the ability to operate effectively across data engineering, AI, and marketing science functions
A proactive, self-starter approach capable of navigating ambiguous data environments and proposing pragmatic solutions
8 years of experience in Data Science or a data engineering-adjacent role
Advanced proficiency in Python and SQL
Deep expertise in Snowflake - including data modeling, Snowpark, and advanced querying
Approximately 2 years of hands-on experience powering agentic AI workflows with data (e.g., RAG pipelines, structured data for LLM tool-calling, agent memory and context layers)
Familiarity with vector databases or embedding-based retrieval systems (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector)
Experience collaborating with or working closely alongside data engineering teams
Familiarity with cloud data platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure)
Snowflake - Snowpark, dynamic tables, advanced SQL querying, data modeling
Python - pandas, NumPy, SQLAlchemy, Jupyter
LLM & Agentic AI Frameworks - LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen, or similar
Vector Databases - Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector, or Chroma
Cloud Platforms - AWS (S3, Lambda, SageMaker), GCP (BigQuery, Vertex AI), or Azure equivalents
Orchestration & Pipeline Tools - dbt, Apache Airflow, or Prefect (as a collaborator/power user)
Version Control - Git/GitHub or GitLab
Data Quality & Observability - Monte Carlo, Great Expectations, or similar
API & Integration Tools - REST APIs, Webhooks, JSON/YAML fluency for connecting data to AI systems
Experience with orchestration tools such as dbt, Airflow, or similar
Exposure to marketing data ecosystems (CDP platforms, ad platform data structures)
Experience in a B2B SaaS environment
Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in a quantitative field such as Computer Science, Statistics, or Data Engineering
description:
About the job

Join Vonage and help us innovate cloud communications for businesses worldwide!

Why This Role Matters

We are looking for a Data Scientist who speaks the language of both data and AI - and knows how to make them work together. You will serve as the critical bridge between our Snowflake data platform and the AI-powered systems that drive our marketing and go-to-market motions. Your work will ensure that our data is shaped, structured, and ready to power the next generation of agentic AI applications, enabling autonomous, intelligent systems that act on behalf of our teams and customers.

Your Key Responsibilities


Collaborate closely with the Snowflake engineering team to define, spec, and validate data requirements for models and AI applications
Serve as the translation layer between data engineering and AI/ML teams, ensuring data is structured, clean, and optimized for downstream use
Enable and support agentic AI workflows by designing and maintaining the data layers that power agent memory, context retrieval, and tool-use
Build and maintain data models and transformation logic on top of Snowflake to support predictive and generative AI use cases
Identify and resolve data quality issues in collaboration with the engineering team
Partner with marketing science and analytics teams to ensure data assets are accessible and model-ready
Automate and streamline data validation processes to improve reliability of AI-facing data pipelines


What You'll Bring


Deep fluency in Snowflake - comfortable living in it daily as a power user, and confident enough to hold technical conversations with Snowflake engineers
A strong understanding of how data must be structured and governed to reliably power AI and machine learning systems
The ability to translate business and modeling needs into precise, actionable data requirements for engineering teams
A collaborative mindset with the ability to operate effectively across data engineering, AI, and marketing science functions
A proactive, self-starter approach capable of navigating ambiguous data environments and proposing pragmatic solutions


Required


8 years of experience in Data Science or a data engineering-adjacent role
Advanced proficiency in Python and SQL
Deep expertise in Snowflake - including data modeling, Snowpark, and advanced querying
Approximately 2 years of hands-on experience powering agentic AI workflows with data (e.g., RAG pipelines, structured data for LLM tool-calling, agent memory and context layers)
Familiarity with vector databases or embedding-based retrieval systems (e.g., Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector)
Experience collaborating with or working closely alongside data engineering teams
Familiarity with cloud data platforms (AWS, GCP, or Azure)


Tools & Technologies


Snowflake - Snowpark, dynamic tables, advanced SQL querying, data modeling
Python - pandas, NumPy, SQLAlchemy, Jupyter
LLM & Agentic AI Frameworks - LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen, or similar
Vector Databases - Pinecone, Weaviate, pgvector, or Chroma
Cloud Platforms - AWS (S3, Lambda, SageMaker), GCP (BigQuery, Vertex AI), or Azure equivalents
Orchestration & Pipeline Tools - dbt, Apache Airflow, or Prefect (as a collaborator/power user)
Version Control - Git/GitHub or GitLab
Data Quality & Observability - Monte Carlo, Great Expectations, or similar
API & Integration Tools - REST APIs, Webhooks, JSON/YAML fluency for connecting data to AI systems


What We Consider a Plus


Experience with orchestration tools such as dbt, Airflow, or similar
Exposure to marketing data ecosystems (CDP platforms, ad platform data structures)
Experience in a B2B SaaS environment
Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in a quantitative field such as Computer Science, Statistics, or Data Engineering


Disclaimer: The posted range represents the good faith salary for this role at the time of posting. Final compensation is determined by factors including (but not limited to) geographic location, relevant experience, specific skill sets, and internal equity.

There’s no perfect candidate. You don't need all the preferred qualifications to make a valuable impact on our team. Our employees and customers come from diverse backgrounds, so if you're passionate about what you could achieve at Vonage, we'd love to hear from you.

To learn how we process your personal data during the recruitment process, please refer to our Privacy Notice.

Who We Are

Vonage is a global cloud communications leader. And your talent will further help brands - such as Airbnb, Viber, WhatsApp, and Snapchat - accelerate their digital transformation through our fully programmable-based unified communications, contact center solutions, and communications APIs. Ready to innovate? Then join us today.

Note: The purpose of this profile is to provide a general summary of essential responsibilities for the position and is not meant as an exhaustive list. Assignments may differ for individuals within the same role based on business conditions, departmental need or geographic location.

title: CRM & Personalization Coordinator
skills: Lead CRM and personalization strategies across consumer lifecycle journeys.
Design scalable, data-driven journeys across owned media channels.
Define audience segmentation and personalization approaches based on consumer behavior and business objectives.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Brand, Media, Analytics, Data, and Tech teams.
Translate business needs into CRM initiatives, journey requirements, and activation plans.
Oversee CRM campaign planning and execution across channels such as Email, Push, SMS, WhatsApp, and Landing Pages.
Monitor CRM performance metrics and identify optimization opportunities.
Drive continuous improvement through testing, insights, and consumer learnings.
Contribute to the evolution of CRM capabilities, governance, and best practices.
Support innovation initiatives related to personalization, automation, and consumer engagement.
4+ years of experience in CRM, Lifecycle Marketing, Personalization, Digital Marketing, or Consumer Engagement roles.
English proficiency required.
Strong experience designing and leading end-to-end CRM strategies and consumer journeys across multiple channels and touchpoints.
Advanced knowledge of Salesforce Marketing Cloud and CRM ecosystems, including journey orchestration, segmentation, automation, and personalization capabilities.
Experience working with Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), audience strategies, first-party data, and consumer lifecycle frameworks.
Strong understanding of data-driven marketing, campaign performance analysis, KPI tracking, and optimization methodologies.
Ability to translate business objectives into scalable CRM and personalization strategies.
Experience collaborating with technical, analytics, media, and brand teams within cross-functional environments.
Strong stakeholder management and leadership skills, with the ability to guide and influence teams and strategic discussions.
Experience managing regional or multi-market CRM initiatives is highly preferred.
Strategic mindset combined with strong execution and organizational capabilities.
Excellent communication, presentation, and problem-solving skills.
Opportunities to learn and develop every day through a wide range of programs.
Internal digital platforms that promote self-learning.
Development programs according to Leadership skills.
Specialized training according to the role.
Learning experiences with internal and external providers.
We love to celebrate success, which is why we have recognition programs for seniority, behavior, leadership, moments of life, among others.
Financial wellness programs that will help you reach your goals in all stages of life.
A flexibility program that will allow you to balance your personal and work life, adapting your working day to your lifestyle.

And because your family is also important to us, they can also enjoy benefits such as our Wellness Line, thousands of Agreements and Discounts, Scholarship programs for your children, Aid Plans for different moments of life, among others.
description:
About the job

Overview

CAREERS TO SMILE ABOUT

At PepsiCo, you’ll discover a place where our mission is to create smiles around the world. With a portfolio of more than 500 beloved brands including, Gatorade, Lay’s and Quaker, our work touches millions of people every day.

At the heart of the company is a team of thinkers, creators, and problem-solvers who collaborate to innovate and turn ideas into action. Driven by innovation and a focus on creating joyful moments through food and drinks, our decisions are guided by consumer centricity, creating opportunities for our associates to do meaningful work and make a lasting impact in the communities we serve.

Whatever your role, you’ll be part of a global community that values your ideas and empowers you to make an impact, on your career and on the world around you.

Responsibilities

The Opportunity

Responsible for leading the strategic design, orchestration, and optimization of CRM and personalized consumer engagement initiatives across owned media channels.

This role sits at the intersection of business, consumer strategy, data, and marketing technology, translating business objectives into connected consumer journeys that drive acquisition, engagement, enrichment, retention, and loyalty foundations.

The role requires a hybrid profile with strong CRM strategy capabilities, consumer-centric thinking, journey orchestration expertise, and a solid understanding of marketing automation and personalization ecosystems such as Salesforce Marketing Cloud and Customer Data Platforms (CDPs).

The CRM & Personalization Coordinator will work cross-functionally with brand teams, analytics, media, data, and technical teams to define scalable CRM strategies and personalized experiences across multiple markets and business units.

Your Impact

As CRM & Personalization Coordinator your scope would consist of …


Lead CRM and personalization strategies across consumer lifecycle journeys.
Design scalable, data-driven journeys across owned media channels.
Define audience segmentation and personalization approaches based on consumer behavior and business objectives.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Brand, Media, Analytics, Data, and Tech teams.
Translate business needs into CRM initiatives, journey requirements, and activation plans.
Oversee CRM campaign planning and execution across channels such as Email, Push, SMS, WhatsApp, and Landing Pages.
Monitor CRM performance metrics and identify optimization opportunities.
Drive continuous improvement through testing, insights, and consumer learnings.
Contribute to the evolution of CRM capabilities, governance, and best practices.
Support innovation initiatives related to personalization, automation, and consumer engagement.


Qualifications

Who Are We Looking For?


4+ years of experience in CRM, Lifecycle Marketing, Personalization, Digital Marketing, or Consumer Engagement roles.
English proficiency required.
Strong experience designing and leading end-to-end CRM strategies and consumer journeys across multiple channels and touchpoints.
Advanced knowledge of Salesforce Marketing Cloud and CRM ecosystems, including journey orchestration, segmentation, automation, and personalization capabilities.
Experience working with Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), audience strategies, first-party data, and consumer lifecycle frameworks.
Strong understanding of data-driven marketing, campaign performance analysis, KPI tracking, and optimization methodologies.
Ability to translate business objectives into scalable CRM and personalization strategies.
Experience collaborating with technical, analytics, media, and brand teams within cross-functional environments.
Strong stakeholder management and leadership skills, with the ability to guide and influence teams and strategic discussions.
Experience managing regional or multi-market CRM initiatives is highly preferred.
Strategic mindset combined with strong execution and organizational capabilities.
Excellent communication, presentation, and problem-solving skills.


If this is an opportunity that interests you, we encourage you to apply even if you do not meet 100% of the requirements.

What can you expect from us: 


Opportunities to learn and develop every day through a wide range of programs. 
Internal digital platforms that promote self-learning. 
Development programs according to Leadership skills. 
Specialized training according to the role. 
Learning experiences with internal and external providers. 
We love to celebrate success, which is why we have recognition programs for seniority, behavior, leadership, moments of life, among others. 
Financial wellness programs that will help you reach your goals in all stages of life. 
A flexibility program that will allow you to balance your personal and work life, adapting your working day to your lifestyle.
And because your family is also important to us, they can also enjoy benefits such as our Wellness Line, thousands of Agreements and Discounts, Scholarship programs for your children, Aid Plans for different moments of life, among others. 


We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We respect and value diversity as a work force and innovation for the organization.

title: Marketing And Public Relations Specialist

Self-starter with strong judgment who can make decisions and recommendations independently
Professional, articulate, and polished presence with clients

Background in finance or financial services
Agency experience

15 days PTO

description:
About the job

This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Marketing Operations Administrator based in Mexico.

This is a high-impact operational role designed for a highly organized professional who thrives in fast-paced, detail-critical environments. You will act as the backbone of marketing operations, ensuring that logistics, budgeting, vendor coordination, and administrative systems run seamlessly across multiple initiatives. Working closely with senior leadership, you will take ownership of day-to-day execution support, freeing up strategic capacity for executive stakeholders. The role spans marketing operations, event coordination, financial tracking, and back-office systems management, requiring both precision and adaptability. You will contribute directly to the smooth execution of events, campaigns, and operational workflows across a multi-brand organization. Ideal for someone who enjoys structure, problem-solving, and ownership, this position combines autonomy with high visibility and real operational impact.

Accountabilities


Act as a direct operational support to senior marketing leadership, managing action items, preparing briefing materials, tracking deliverables, and ensuring follow-through on priorities.
Oversee marketing financial operations including budget tracking, invoice processing, vendor payments, expense reconciliation, and maintenance of spend and SaaS cost controls.
Manage end-to-end inventory, print, and fulfillment processes, including sourcing branded materials, coordinating shipments, tracking delivery timelines, and overseeing stock management.
Coordinate procurement and vendor management activities, ensuring contracts, invoices, and service agreements are properly tracked and maintained.
Support back-office operations including document management, scheduling coordination, access provisioning, and administrative system upkeep.
Plan and execute marketing events and conferences, including logistics such as venue booking, catering, travel arrangements, booth setup, AV coordination, and on-site execution.
Support internal teams by ensuring operational readiness across campaigns, events, and marketing initiatives with no disruption to timelines or deliverables.
Maintain operational dashboards, spreadsheets, and tracking systems to ensure full visibility over marketing execution and financial flows.


Requirements


7+ years of experience in marketing operations, executive support, operations management, or a logistics-heavy administrative role with ownership of budgets and projects.
Strong experience in event planning and execution, including conferences, booths, vendor coordination, travel logistics, and on-site operations.
Solid understanding of financial operations such as invoice processing, expense reconciliation, GL coding, and budget vs. actual tracking.
Advanced proficiency in Google Workspace, spreadsheets, and operational tools; familiarity with SaaS systems, billing processes, and digital workflows.
Experience managing procurement, vendor relationships, print production, fulfillment, and shipping logistics.
Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple deadlines without losing accuracy or control over execution.
Strong ownership mindset with a proactive, self-sufficient approach to problem-solving and task execution.
Comfortable leveraging AI tools and automation to improve workflows, streamline operations, and enhance efficiency.
Strong written and spoken English communication skills, with the ability to work effectively in a remote international environment.
Valid passport and willingness to travel internationally for on-site events (up to several times per year).
Experience supporting senior executives or acting in an executive assistant / operations hybrid capacity is highly desirable.


Benefits


Fully remote working arrangement based in LATAM with flexible coordination across international teams.
Competitive monthly compensation of USD $2,500.
Opportunity to work closely with senior leadership in a high-ownership operational role.
Exposure to large-scale marketing operations, real estate investment branding, and legacy-focused initiatives.
Hands-on involvement in end-to-end event execution, including international conferences and on-site activations.
Access to modern operational tools and AI-driven workflows to improve efficiency and automation.
Professional growth through high-responsibility work and direct executive exposure.
Occasional international travel for events and conferences.
Opportunity to build and optimize real operational systems with tangible business impact.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

 Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

description:
About the job

Job Title: Entry Level Software Developer (Remote)
Work Mode: Fully Remote
Schedule: Flexible (up to 40 hours per week, based on project availability)




Role Overview:

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Review and evaluate code written by AI models for accuracy, efficiency, and best practices
Rate AI responses across key dimensions including correctness, instruction-following, and quality
Write original computer science questions and coding challenges to test AI capabilities
Identify bugs, errors, and inconsistencies in AI-generated code using structured guidelines
Provide clear, constructive, and structured written feedback to improve AI responses

title: CRO Manager
skills: Lead the development and execution of A/B tests, multivariate tests, and personalization strategies to improve conversion rates and optimize user experiences.
Conduct and analyze user research, heatmaps, session recordings, and surveys to identify drop-off points and pain points in the user journey.
Develop and implement a comprehensive CRO strategy focused on increasing conversions across all digital platforms, including web, mobile, and app interfaces.
Work closely with UX/UI designers, developers, and content teams to create and execute on optimization plans. Ensure changes are effectively implemented and tracked.
Use tools such as Google Analytics, Hotjar, and other CRO platforms to measure, analyze, and report on the effectiveness of experiments.
Apply a user-centered approach to solving design and usability challenges, creating smooth and effective user flows that drive desired outcomes.
Continuously refine the CRO process, advocating for best practices in testing and optimization within the organization.
Work closely with stakeholders from marketing, sales, and product teams in an agile environment, ensuring alignment on goals and strategies.
Minimum 3-4 years of experience in a CRO or digital optimization role, with a proven track record of improving conversion rates.
Strong understanding of A/B testing, multivariate testing, and personalization techniques. Expertise in using testing platforms (e.g., Optimizely, VWO) and analytical tools like Google Analytics.
Ability to draw actionable insights from user behavior data, and translate these insights into optimization plans.
Comfortable with the technical aspects of web analytics, basic HTML/CSS, and JavaScript, with the ability to communicate requirements to developers.
Understanding of UX/UI principles and how design impacts conversion rates.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present insights and recommendations to cross-functional teams.
High Ownership: Lead growth across a $100M+ brand platform
Creative Autonomy: Big sandbox, bold ideas encouraged
Cross-Brand Impact: Learn and scale across multiple industries and audiences
Fast Execution: We move quickly and prioritize performance
description:
About the job

About Pathfinder

Pathfinder is a brand platform on a mission to acquire, scale, and revitalize exceptional consumer brands in the U.S. Backed by a committed capital vehicle and led by founders with experience spanning Stanford GSB, Harvard Law, Y Combinator, Forbes 30U30, and deep expertise in e-commerce, marketing, and finance, we acquire high-potential DTC brands and unlock growth through hands-on operational excellence. We have acquired several brands to-date and are building a dream team of experts who want to turn previously mismanaged brands into breakout successes.

Our long-term vision: build a $100M+ portfolio of beloved, performance-driven consumer brands.




About the Role

We’re hiring an exceptional CRO Manager to turn traffic into purchases for 2 in-house DTC brands.

In this remote role, you will own and execute conversion optimization strategies to maximize website performance and marketing funnel efficiency. 




You’ll work closely with the Head of Growth, Design, and web development teams to create data-driven experiments, implement UX/UI improvements, and drive measurable conversion rate growth. 




What You’ll Do: 

Lead the development and execution of A/B tests, multivariate tests, and personalization strategies to improve conversion rates and optimize user experiences.
Conduct and analyze user research, heatmaps, session recordings, and surveys to identify drop-off points and pain points in the user journey.
Develop and implement a comprehensive CRO strategy focused on increasing conversions across all digital platforms, including web, mobile, and app interfaces.
Work closely with UX/UI designers, developers, and content teams to create and execute on optimization plans. Ensure changes are effectively implemented and tracked.
Use tools such as Google Analytics, Hotjar, and other CRO platforms to measure, analyze, and report on the effectiveness of experiments.
Apply a user-centered approach to solving design and usability challenges, creating smooth and effective user flows that drive desired outcomes.
Continuously refine the CRO process, advocating for best practices in testing and optimization within the organization.
Work closely with stakeholders from marketing, sales, and product teams in an agile environment, ensuring alignment on goals and strategies.




Skills, Knowledge, and Experience:
Minimum 3-4 years of experience in a CRO or digital optimization role, with a proven track record of improving conversion rates.
Strong understanding of A/B testing, multivariate testing, and personalization techniques. Expertise in using testing platforms (e.g., Optimizely, VWO) and analytical tools like Google Analytics.
Ability to draw actionable insights from user behavior data, and translate these insights into optimization plans.
Comfortable with the technical aspects of web analytics, basic HTML/CSS, and JavaScript, with the ability to communicate requirements to developers.
Understanding of UX/UI principles and how design impacts conversion rates.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present insights and recommendations to cross-functional teams.




Why Join Pathfinder?

High Ownership: Lead growth across a $100M+ brand platform
Creative Autonomy: Big sandbox, bold ideas encouraged
Cross-Brand Impact: Learn and scale across multiple industries and audiences
Fast Execution: We move quickly and prioritize performance

description:
About the job

Condé Nast is a global media company producing the highest quality content with a footprint of more than 1 billion consumers in 32 territories through print, digital, video and social platforms. The company’s portfolio includes many of the world’s most respected and influential media properties including Vogue, Vanity Fair, Glamour, Self, GQ, The New Yorker, Condé Nast Traveler/Traveller, Allure, AD, Bon Appétit and Wired, among others.

Job Description

Location:

Ciudad de México, CMX

Condé Nast is a global media company, home to iconic brands including Vogue, The New Yorker, GQ, Glamour, AD, Vanity Fair and Wired, among many others. The company's award-winning content reaches 84 million consumers in print, 367 million in digital and 379 million across social platforms, and generates more than 1 billion video views each month.

The company is headquartered in London and New York, and operates in 32 markets worldwide, including China, France, Germany, India, Italy, Japan, Mexico & Latin America, Spain, Taiwan, the UK and the USA, with local license partners across the globe.

What we’re looking for:

A Workday Specialist to support the critical work of maintaining and evolving our Workday platform within a full tenant implementation (Finance, U.S. Payroll and HCM). You will work within a broader Workday team that supports key business objectives by working with business stakeholders, project leads, and cross functional technology teams to build, test and deliver key technology efforts. The ideal candidate has strong communication and interpersonal skills and enjoys working in a dynamic, global, team-oriented environment along with keen attention to detail and the ability to collaborate with a diverse group of stakeholders across various business functions.

Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:


General understanding of Workday security
Foundational knowledge to configure Workday business processes
Ability to develop and maintain standard Workday reports
Partner with stakeholders and senior Workday Specialists to understand requirements and ensure results meet the business needs
Leverage knowledge of customer support principals, and best practices to provide best in class service to our internal customers
Engage with vendor support as needed for support related to the platform
Prioritize work against priorities and dependencies using team processes and standards
Work with product managers and senior specialists to review Workday business processes, and evaluate against new features and functionality with an eye to increased automation and reduction of manual process
Support new Workday release activities relating to the needs of the business and impact assessment


Essential Skills & Requirements:


1+ years supporting Workday or similar experiences in other ERPs
Ability to take instruction, prioritize and execute tasks in a dynamic, flexible environment
Foundational knowledge with ticketing systems and enterprise collaboration tools
Strong interpersonal skills and customer service orientation
Highly self-motivated, self-directed, and attentive to detail
Ability to work with cross-functional teams


What happens next?

If you are interested in this opportunity, please apply below, and we will review your application as soon as possible. You can update your resume or upload a cover letter at any time by accessing your candidate profile.

Condé Nast is an equal opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, age, familial status and other legally protected characteristics.
