description:
About the job

Who we are

About Stripe

Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career.

About The Team

At Stripe, our Mexico City office is a vibrant hub at the forefront of our mission to reshape the financial landscape for businesses worldwide. Our team prioritizes collaboration, innovation, and excellence. As a member of the Mexico City team, you'll be part of a mission-driven community dedicated to enhancing the global economy and increasing the GDP of the internet. We strive for excellence by creating with craft and beauty, while having fun and celebrating our successes together.

We cultivate a culture of collaboration, inclusivity, and support where every team member’s voice matters. Our commitment shines through as we handle over a million support cases each year, empowering our users not just to solve problems but to achieve their goals.

What you’ll do

Responsibilities


Troubleshoot and solve external user issues 
Analyze our processes and instigate changes to help scale our operations and improve user experience 
Work closely with other Stripe teams to improve our product offerings and messaging around new products and features 
Gain unique insights into how thousands of businesses on Stripe scale and operate 
Have an opportunity to learn how Stripe operates from the ground up and make a real impact on the future of the organization 
Be a part of building a brand-new team and operational culture for Stripe 
Partner with global operations specialists or business process owners to run a critical workflow



Who you are

We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.

Minimum Requirements


You have a user-first mindset and are energized by the challenge of solving difficult problems
You have excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
You excel in critical thinking and problem solving
You have prior experience in customer service or internal and external user-facing operations
You enjoy learning about technical products and processes, you're an organized, self-starter with passion for finding solutions to our user’s challenges
You have a process-oriented mindset and the ability to get things done
You enjoy working in an in-office environment with strong cross-team collaboration and support
You prioritize well and enjoy working in a quick-moving environment
You are humble and have a proven track record for working well across teams and with external partners
You're willing to periodically work a weekend day for which you'll receive a weekday off. The SDC operates during daytime hours with shifts from the morning through early evening



Preferred Qualifications


Prior experience or knowledge in user support 
Prior experience working on projects or process improvement initiatives 



In-office expectations

Office-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible.

Pay and benefits

Stripe does not yet include pay ranges in job postings in every country. Stripe strongly values pay transparency and is working toward pay transparency globally.

description:
About the job

Acerca de nosotros:

Grupo Innovasport es una empresa de retail apasionada por impactar positivamente la calidad de vida de las personas a través del deporte. Con 27 años de trayectoria, cuenta con más de 270 sucursales en 27 estados y su equipo está conformado por más de 3,600 jugadores, operando bajo los banners: Innovasport, Innvictus, Culto, Overtime e Innergy.




Sobre el rol:

Como App Operations Analyst, asegurarás que nuestra app mantenga una operación estable y disponible durante el día a día comercial. Monitorearás señales críticas, coordinarás la atención de incidentes, validarás releases, cambios y ejecutarás configuraciones comerciales dentro de la aplicación para reducir fricciones que afecten la navegación, promociones, redención de beneficios y experiencia de compra.




Ubicación y esquema de trabajo:

Híbrido en Polanco, CDMX o San Pedro Garza García, N.L.




¿Qué debes hacer para llegar a la meta?

Monitorear el desempeño, disponibilidad y navegación de la app para identificar alertas, desviaciones o afectaciones antes de que escalen.
Gestionar incidentes desde su detección hasta el cierre, coordinando análisis, seguimiento, comunicación y validación de la solución con los equipos involucrados.
Coordinar y validar despliegues, cambios y liberaciones en producción, asegurando que se ejecuten conforme a los procesos establecidos.
Analizar métricas operativas para detectar oportunidades de mejora en estabilidad, desempeño y experiencia de usuario.
Dar seguimiento a indicadores de servicio, disponibilidad y cumplimiento de acuerdos con equipos de Desarrollo, Infraestructura, Producto y Negocio.
Configurar planes promocionales, monitorear la correcta redención de beneficios y atender desviaciones que impacten la ejecución comercial.
Ejecutar cambios en componentes visuales de la app de acuerdo con el plan de venta y las prioridades del negocio.




¿Qué debes dominar?

2+ años de experiencia en operación de canales digitales transaccionales, como eCommerce, sitio web y/o aplicaciones móviles.
Dominio de Excel para análisis, control y seguimiento de información.
Enfoque data-driven para analizar métricas operativas y comerciales y convertir hallazgos en acciones de mejora.
Licenciatura / Ingeniería terminada.
Orientación a resultados, dinamismo y mentalidad de mejora continua.

title: Marketing Strategy Consultant (PowerPoint Strategy) (Remote)
skills: Role: Marketing Strategy Consultant (PowerPoint Strategy) (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: Competitive, based on experience
description:
About the job

Role: Marketing Strategy Consultant (PowerPoint Strategy) (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: Competitive, based on experience




Role Overview:

This role involves creating and refining PowerPoint presentations to support strategic marketing initiatives for clients in the technology and artificial intelligence sectors. You will translate complex market analysis, competitive insights, and business recommendations into clear, visually compelling decks for executive audiences. The work directly influences high-stakes business decisions, ensuring stakeholders can act on data-driven insights efficiently.




Key Responsibilities:

• Develop high-impact PowerPoint presentations tailored to marketing strategy briefs for AI and tech clients.

• Structure presentations to highlight key findings, market trends, and strategic recommendations with data visualization.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather content, refine messaging, and ensure alignment with business objectives.

• Incorporate feedback from stakeholders to iterate and finalize presentation materials for client delivery.

• Maintain consistency in design, branding, and storytelling across multiple presentation versions.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Expert-level proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint with strong design and layout skills for business presentations.

• 3+ years of experience creating marketing or business strategy presentations for technology or AI-focused audiences.

• Ability to distill complex technical concepts into clear, audience-appropriate visual narratives using charts, diagrams, and infographics.

• Experience working with market research, competitive analysis, or strategic consulting deliverables in a remote setting.

• Strong communication skills to coordinate with teams and integrate feedback efficiently.




More About the Opportunity:

This role supports a global leader in AI and technology marketing, contributing to high-visibility client projects that shape industry strategies. You will have the flexibility to work from anywhere while delivering presentations that drive measurable business outcomes. The scope includes short-term contracts with potential for ongoing engagement based on project needs.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.




Apply Now!

Salary Range

Job Posting Title:

Manager

The NBA does not accept unsolicited resumes from search firms or any other third parties. Any unsolicited resume sent to the NBA will be considered NBA property, and the NBA will not pay a fee should it hire the subject of any unsolicited resume.

The NBA considers applicants for all positions on the basis of merit, qualifications, and business needs, and without regard to race, color, national origin, religion, sex, age, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, alienage or citizenship status, ancestry, marital status, genetic predisposition or carrier status, veteran status, familial status, status as a victim of domestic violence, or any other status or characteristic protected by applicable federal, state, or local law.

The NBA is committed to providing a safe and healthy workplace. To safeguard our employees and their families, our visitors, and the broader community from COVID-19, and in consideration of recommendations from health authorities and the NBA’s own advisors, any individual working onsite in our New York and New Jersey offices must be fully vaccinated against COVID-19. The NBA will discuss accommodations for individuals who cannot be vaccinated due to a medical reason or sincerely held religious belief, practice, or observance.

About The NBA

The National Basketball Association (NBA) is a global sports and media organization with the mission to inspire and connect people everywhere through the power of basketball.  Built around five professional sports leagues:  the NBA, WNBA, NBA G League, NBA 2K League and Basketball Africa League, the NBA has established a major international presence with games and programming available in 215 countries and territories in more than 50 languages, and merchandise for sale in more than 200 countries and territories on all seven continents.  NBA rosters at the start of the 2021-22 season featured a record 121 international players from 40 countries.  NBA Digital’s assets include NBA TV, NBA.com, the NBA App and NBA League Pass.  The NBA has created one of the largest social media communities in the world, with 2.1 billion likes and followers globally across all league, team, and player platforms.  Through NBA Cares, the league addresses important social issues by working with internationally recognized youth-serving organizations that support education, youth and family development, and health-related causes.

description:
About the job

About Charly Agency

 Charly Agency is a growing digital agency specializing in web design, UX/UI, branding, ecommerce, and development for clients primarily in the US. Operating globally with offices in San Francisco and Barcelona, we partner with brands ranging from startups to enterprises. Our team is spread across Europe and Latin America and we are scaling fast.




About the Role

 We are looking for a freelance copywriter to join our pool of creative collaborators. You will write across brand, web, and marketing, working closely with our design and strategy team to bring client projects to life through sharp, purposeful copy.

This is not a beginner role. We want someone who can take a brief, understand the business behind it, and produce copy that is clear, purposeful, and built to convert — without hand-holding. Mistakes happen, but high quality, great communication, and honesty are non-negotiable. No vague communication, no hiding behind "I was waiting for feedback."




What You'll Do

Write website copy across homepages, landing pages, product pages, and about pages
Develop brand voice guidelines and messaging frameworks
Write case studies, blog posts, and marketing content
Collaborate with designers to ensure copy and design work together




What We're Looking For

Strong portfolio of web and brand copywriting
Excellent written English, this is non-negotiable
Experience writing for DTC, SaaS, B2B, or CPG brands is a plus
Comfortable delivering quickly under tight deadlines without sacrificing quality
Clear and thoughtful approach to using AI in your workflow
Reliable, communicative, and comfortable working async
Eager to learn, open to direction, and quick on feedback
Agency experience is a strong plus




About Us

 Charly is a creative agency with a warm, international team that takes the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously. We collaborate across time zones, support each other, and care a lot about doing great work for clients we genuinely like. If you are kind, talented, and ambitious, you will fit right in.




To Apply, send:

Your strongest copywriting samples (website, landing page, or brand copy preferred)
Examples of projects you wrote for and your role in them
Your availability and preferred working hours
Your expected rate (hourly or per project)

description:
About the job

About Klar

Let’s start with the basics! Klar is a Mexican fintech whose mission is to democratize and revolutionize how financial services are delivered in Mexico — especially since half of the population still doesn’t have a bank account. Since our launch in 2019, we’ve served more than 7 million users with a growing suite of debit and credit products, and the future is brighter than ever.

Today, we’re not only scaling our consumer business, but also building Klar Empresas (B2B) from the ground up — a new division focused on empowering Mexican SMEs with the financial tools, capital, and services they need to grow. This is an exciting new chapter where we get to design and build from scratch while leveraging the trust and scale of Klar’s platform.

We are also proud to have been certified as a “Great Place to Work” in Mexico — something we’ve worked hard to achieve and will continue to build on.




Our people

If you join us at Klar, you’ll be welcomed to a team which is rich in many talents and we are very proud! With our head office in Mexico City, and remote tech hubs in Berlin and Argentina, we are always learning something new about another culture or language. With so many people from different backgrounds and walks of life (young professionals, parents, LGBTQ+, neurodivergence), you’ll definitely find your people here!




Our values

Ownership - We own our successes & our failures as a team.
Excellence - We do everything to the best of our ability & always seek to achieve a new level of excellence in our work.
Inclusion - We believe we are stronger together and actively work to promote a safe, diverse, inclusive, and respectful culture.
Customer Obsession - We understand the value Klar can bring to its customers & it’s always at the forefront of our decisions.
Klarity - We communicate clearly & with authenticity. It’s in our name & it’s what we do.




The position and your daily adventures

We’re looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist to execute, optimize, and scale paid acquisition campaigns across Google, Meta, and other performance platforms. This is a hands-on role for someone who loves data, experimentation, and turning budgets into measurable growth.

You will own the day-to-day operation of performance campaigns, continuously improving CAC, ROAS, and funnel conversion, working closely with creative, analytics, and growth teams. You’ll be part of a high-performance team managing a US$2M+ monthly budget.







What you can expect:

Reporting: Create weekly and ad-hoc performance reports. Make financial analysis of campaign spend.
Own campaign tracking, attribution setup, and pixel/event health.
Operate and optimize paid campaigns: Constant monitoring and daily adjustments of paid ads campaigns on Google, Facebook and new channels.
Launch new campaigns, ad sets, creatives, and targeting strategies
Monitor performance daily and implement data-driven optimizations.
Run A/B tests on creatives, audiences, landing pages, and funnels.
Collaborate with creative and product teams to improve conversion.
KEY KPIs: New users and approved credit lines, Cost per Acquisition (CpA), and ROAS.




What we are looking for:

Bachelor's degree in Industrial Engineering, Business Engineering, Digital Transformation Engineering, (or similar) with an orientation to data analysis.
2+ years hands-on experience running performance campaigns on: Meta and Google.
Strong analytical skills, comfort working with performance dashboards and experience with SQL.
Experience with conversion tracking, pixels, and attribution tools
Ability to do Data-driven decision-making
High ownership, attention to detail, and execution speed
Advanced English level
Experience in fintech, apps, or subscription products is a plus




Our offer to you:

Competitive salary based on performance and experience
Chance of earning Klar stock options
15 days of paid vacation per year; plus extended maternity and paternity leaves
Vacation premium
Food vouchers
30 days of Christmas bonus
Medical Insurance
Computer devices
Well hub subscription to offer mental and physical health
Sponsored coaching and therapy sessions
A modern centrally located office in Mexico City with free drinks, snacks, and regular social events
International work environment with amazing and highly skilled people
A world-class team that helps you evolve your skills in areas you're interested in




Klar is a safe place for everyone!

We trust our highly skilled and diverse team, and we’re committed to creating a welcoming and inclusive environment for new talents to flourish. We value diversity and welcome all applications regardless of gender, nationality, ethnic and social origin, religion/belief, physical abilities, age, sexual orientation and identity.

Should you require any accommodations through the recruitment process, please don’t hesitate to let us know how we can help!

description:
About the job

Role Overview




We are hiring an Influencer Growth Specialist to support a portfolio of content creators across audience growth, content strategy, creator operations, and monetization initiatives.

This is not a traditional influencer marketing role. We are looking for someone with hands-on experience working directly with creators to help them grow their audiences, strengthen their personal brands, optimize content performance, and expand revenue opportunities across today's creator economy.

The ideal candidate understands how creators build sustainable businesses through content strategy, audience engagement, platform optimization, and multiple monetization channels.




Salary: $2,000 USD per month




Key Responsibilities

Manage a portfolio of content creators as their primary point of contact.
Develop and execute audience growth strategies across TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and emerging creator platforms.
Advise creators on content planning, performance optimization, audience engagement, and platform best practices.
Create and manage content calendars to support consistent publishing.
Assist with copywriting, editing, scheduling, publishing, and content organization.
Analyze creator performance and provide data-driven recommendations to improve growth.
Monitor platform updates, creator economy trends, and emerging opportunities.
Identify opportunities to increase audience engagement, reach, follower growth, and content performance.
Support creator monetization through TikTok Shop, affiliate marketing, brand partnerships, LTK, social commerce, YouTube monetization, and other revenue channels.
Help creators identify new monetization opportunities aligned with their audience and content niche.
Track campaigns, partnerships, affiliate programs, and creator performance within internal systems.
Maintain accurate records in CRM platforms, Monday.com, and other operational tools.
Coordinate communication between creators, internal teams, and brand partners.
Support partnership administration, campaign tracking, invoicing, and operational processes.




Requirements

Resume in English.
Native or near-native English proficiency (spoken and written).
3+ years of experience in creator talent management, creator partnerships, creator success, creator operations, or the creator economy.
Proven experience working directly with content creators, influencers, personal brands, or creator rosters.
Demonstrated success helping creators grow audiences, improve content performance, and increase monetization.
Strong understanding of creator business models and the creator economy.
Deep knowledge of TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and emerging creator platforms.
Experience developing content strategies, growth plans, and audience engagement initiatives.
Strong copywriting, content planning, and content optimization skills.
Experience analyzing creator performance metrics and turning insights into actionable recommendations.
Experience supporting creator monetization through affiliate marketing, TikTok Shop, brand partnerships, social commerce, LTK, YouTube monetization, subscriptions, or similar revenue channels.
Experience using CRM platforms, Monday.com, or similar project management tools.
Strong organizational, communication, and relationship management skills.
Comfortable managing multiple creators and priorities simultaneously in a fast-paced environment.

description:
About the job

We are seeking a highly organized, detail-oriented Partner Marketing Coordinator to support the execution of Weave’s partner marketing programs and initiatives.

This role is execution-focused and plays a critical part in ensuring campaigns, communications, and partner initiatives are delivered on time, accurately, and with high quality. The Coordinator will manage day-to-day project workflows, support partner communications, and maintain systems and processes that keep programs running smoothly.

The ideal candidate thrives in a fast-paced environment, is exceptionally detail-oriented, and is comfortable managing multiple projects while coordinating across internal teams and external partners.


This position will be remote in Mexico 
Reports to: Marketing Director 


What You Will Own


 Project Management & Execution (Primary Ownership)
Manage day-to-day execution of partner marketing initiatives, ensuring all projects are delivered on time and aligned with objectives
Build and maintain project plans, timelines, and task tracking across multiple campaigns and programs
Coordinate cross-functional workflows between marketing, sales, creative, and partner teams
Track progress against deadlines and proactively follow up to ensure tasks are completed
Identify risks, bottlenecks, or delays early and communicate updates to stakeholders
Ensure all deliverables meet quality standards and are completed with accuracy
 Partner Communications & Coordination
Support day-to-day communication with partner marketing counterparts, ensuring timely and clear responses
Coordinate campaign details, timelines, and deliverables with partners
Assist in drafting and sending partner communications, including emails, updates, and campaign materials
Maintain organized records of partner communications and engagement history
Support distribution of marketing assets, toolkits, and resources to partners
Help ensure partners have what they need to successfully execute campaigns
 Campaign & Content Coordination
Coordinate execution of co-marketing campaigns across channels (email, events, webinars, digital, etc.)
Manage timelines and delivery of campaign assets, ensuring alignment with brand and messaging guidelines
Track content usage and ensure assets are properly organized and accessible
Support updates and maintenance of partner-facing and internal marketing materials
Assist in adapting or formatting content for partner use (slides, one-pagers, emails, etc.)
 Program Tracking & Documentation
Maintain accurate and up-to-date tracking systems for campaigns, partners, and deliverables
Ensure all project details, timelines, and statuses are clearly documented and accessible
Track partner program activity, campaign participation, and execution status
Maintain organized documentation across tools and systems to support visibility and alignment
Support onboarding documentation and process guides for partner programs
 Reporting & Performance Support
Assist in gathering and organizing data related to campaign and partner performance
Maintain reporting trackers for key metrics such as leads, engagement, and pipeline contribution
Support preparation of recurring reports and summaries for internal stakeholders
Ensure data accuracy and consistency across reporting systems
Help identify trends or gaps based on performance data


What You Will Need to Accomplish the Job


1+ years of experience in marketing coordination, project management, or similar roles
Strong organizational and project management skills
Exceptional attention to detail and accuracy
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Ability to manage multiple projects and deadlines simultaneously
Basic experience with marketing, project management and CRM tools


What Will Make Us Love You


Basic skills in email/HTML, Adobe Creative Suite, Canva, WordPress or similar to support creative development
Experience supporting partner, channel, or co-marketing programs
Familiarity with campaign execution across multiple marketing channels
Experience with budget management and campaign performance reporting

description:
About the job

Overview

CAREERS TO SMILE ABOUT 

At PepsiCo, you’ll discover a place where our mission is to create smiles around the world. With a portfolio of more than 500 beloved brands including, Gatorade, Lay’s and Quaker, our work touches millions of people every day.

At the heart of the company is a team of thinkers, creators, and problem-solvers who collaborate to innovate and turn ideas into action. Driven by innovation and a focus on creating joyful moments through food and drinks, our decisions are guided by consumer centricity, creating opportunities for our associates to do meaningful work and make a lasting impact in the communities we serve.

Whatever your role, you’ll be part of a global community that values your ideas and empowers you to make an impact, on your career and on the world around you.

Responsibilities

The Opportunity 

As a One Touch & Process Automation Analyst, the candidate will gain in-depth exposure to end-to-end purchasing operations for beverage products while supporting the U.S. beverage market. The position offers a unique opportunity to contribute to key operational processes, including invoice validation and Proof of Delivery (POD) reviews, while driving impactful process improvement initiatives.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone passionate about automation, data analysis, and continuous improvement. The selected candidate will play a key role in identifying inefficiencies, developing automation solutions, and enhancing daily operational processes that directly support business performance.

Your Impact 


Develop and implement automation solutions to improve purchasing processes and operational efficiency.
Manage inventory records and maintain purchasing and inventory reports to support business decisions.
Review invoices and Proof of Delivery (POD) documentation to ensure accuracy and compliance.
Investigate and resolve delivery discrepancies by coordinating with carriers and Customer Care teams.
Identify opportunities for process improvement and support continuous improvement initiatives across purchasing operations.


Qualifications

Who Are We Looking For? 


Experience in process improvement and operational efficiency initiatives.
Fluent English (written and spoken).
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint.
Knowledge of Microsoft Power Platform (Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power Pages).
Ability to learn and navigate multiple systems and platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Proactive, curious, and self-driven mindset.
Excellent teamwork and communication skills.
Knowledge of programming languages or scripting tools is a plus, but not required.


If this is an opportunity that interests you, we encourage you to apply even if you do not meet 100% of the requirements.

What can you expect from us: 


Opportunities to learn and develop every day through a wide range of programs. 
Internal digital platforms that promote self-learning. 
Development programs according to Leadership skills. 
Specialized training according to the role. 
Learning experiences with internal and external providers. 
We love to celebrate success, which is why we have recognition programs for seniority, behavior, leadership, moments of life, among others. 
Financial wellness programs that will help you reach your goals in all stages of life. 
A flexibility program that will allow you to balance your personal and work life, adapting your working day to your lifestyle. 
And because your family is also important to us, they can also enjoy benefits such as our Wellness Line, thousands of Agreements and Discounts, Scholarship programs for your children, Aid Plans for different moments of life, among others. 


We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We respect and value diversity as a work force and innovation for the organization.

title: E-commerce Simulation Expert (Remote)
skills: Role: E-commerce Simulation Expert
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: $40 - $50/hour
description:
About the job

Role: E-commerce Simulation Expert
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: $40 - $50/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking an E-commerce Simulation Expert to work on a contract basis. The role focuses on building and refining synthetic e-commerce environments that train and evaluate advanced AI models. Your expertise in real-world commerce will directly shape how AI systems learn, reason, and perform across critical domains. This work supports the development of reliable AI agents and contributes to the evolution of human-in-the-loop AI training.




Key Responsibilities:

• Design and construct synthetic digital stores with realistic product assortments, logical category structures, and authentic pricing models to reflect real-world e-commerce complexity.

• Generate, structure, and maintain comprehensive product catalogs ensuring high data quality, logical consistency, and alignment with market standards.

• Develop diverse shopping scenarios and user prompts that simulate varied consumer intents, preferences, and challenging edge cases to stress-test AI behavior.

• Create evaluation rubrics and conduct rigorous quality assurance to ensure generated data and simulations meet domain-specific accuracy and realism requirements.

• Collaborate with AI teams to refine prompt engineering and scenario design for continuous improvement of AI training datasets.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Demonstrated experience in e-commerce operations, including product data management, catalog structuring, or retail systems with a focus on data integrity and real-world relevance.

• Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills to design complex, logically consistent product hierarchies and pricing logic.

• Familiarity with AI/LLM prompting and evaluation principles, or ability to quickly learn how to structure inputs that generate high-quality training data for AI systems.

• Background in scenario design and user behavior modeling, preferably in retail, consumer goods, or related domains.

• Attention to detail and a quality assurance mindset to maintain high standards in synthetic data generation and simulation environments.

• Self-directed work style with ability to manage multiple asynchronous tasks and deliver consistent, high-quality output under defined deadlines.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers the chance to contribute directly to the development of frontier AI systems by leveraging real-world e-commerce expertise. You will work within a global network supporting AI research, with exposure to cutting-edge reinforcement learning environments and agent-based AI training. The position is ideal for professionals seeking meaningful impact through applied domain knowledge in a rapidly evolving field.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.




Apply Now!

title: Deal Strategist - CDMX
skills: Manage end-to-end customer deals—drafting, editing, and reviewing agreements—while supporting the sales cycle to ensure deal terms align with business goals, reflect strategic priorities, and close as forecasted.
Act as the primary point of contact between Sales, Legal, and other cross-functional stakeholders for deal-related questions, approvals, and escalations.
Work directly with sales teams, sales leaders, services/support teams, and customers to advise on deal structures, establish compelling commercials, and drive resolution of customer issues.
Advise sales teams on the best approach by understanding customer and partner requirements, while balancing custom terms with internal system constraints.
Remove internal blockers and drive alignment across cross-functional teams including Product, Legal, Finance, and Accounting.
Support complex negotiations involving bespoke commercial and legal agreements.
Support the pricing and structuring of multi-threaded product use cases in all segments and industries, especially in highly competitive sales cycles.
Quickly build credibility and trust with internal stakeholders.
Demonstrate strong communication and persuasion skills when working with cross-functional partners and senior leaders.
BA/BS degree in Business, Technology, Finance, or Economics
6+ years of experience in deal strategy, procurement, pricing strategy, financial consulting, or legal
Experience working in ambiguous, fast-scaling environments and comfortable operating with high agency
Strong communication, presentation, negotiation, and project management skills
Experience working in Payments and/or SaaS
description:
About the job

Who we are

About Stripe

Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world's largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone's reach while doing the most important work of your career.

About The Team

GBS's primary goal is to ensure operational excellence across deal cycles, increase revenue through the development of transformative commercial constructs, and drive closure of Stripe's deal portfolio. The team's reach is widespread across Stripe, not exclusive to one sales segment or geographical region; GBS drives change across go-to-market, positively impacting Stripe users and cross-functional teams. GBS's priorities are set through the direction of Stripe leadership and executive vision, partnership with sales and cross-functional organizations, as well as user feedback and market insights.

What you'll do

Stripe's Global Business Services (GBS) team is looking for a Deal Strategist to focus on the closure of custom and standard commercial deals. You will be responsible for deal lifecycle management and supporting cross-organizational alignment. This role requires a background in driving custom deals to closure, a strong understanding of pricing frameworks, and experience supporting sales through negotiations.

The candidate will have experience with SaaS products, pricing strategies, and supporting selling motions, along with strong communication skills. You will love this job if you are a natural relationship builder and process architect.

Responsibilities


Manage end-to-end customer deals—drafting, editing, and reviewing agreements—while supporting the sales cycle to ensure deal terms align with business goals, reflect strategic priorities, and close as forecasted.
Act as the primary point of contact between Sales, Legal, and other cross-functional stakeholders for deal-related questions, approvals, and escalations.
Work directly with sales teams, sales leaders, services/support teams, and customers to advise on deal structures, establish compelling commercials, and drive resolution of customer issues.
Advise sales teams on the best approach by understanding customer and partner requirements, while balancing custom terms with internal system constraints.
Remove internal blockers and drive alignment across cross-functional teams including Product, Legal, Finance, and Accounting.
Support complex negotiations involving bespoke commercial and legal agreements.
Support the pricing and structuring of multi-threaded product use cases in all segments and industries, especially in highly competitive sales cycles.
Quickly build credibility and trust with internal stakeholders.
Demonstrate strong communication and persuasion skills when working with cross-functional partners and senior leaders.



Who you are

We're looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply.

Minimum Requirements


BA/BS degree in Business, Technology, Finance, or Economics
6+ years of experience in deal strategy, procurement, pricing strategy, financial consulting, or legal
Experience working in ambiguous, fast-scaling environments and comfortable operating with high agency
Strong communication, presentation, negotiation, and project management skills
Experience working in Payments and/or SaaS



In-office expectations

Office-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible.

Pay and benefits

Stripe does not yet include pay ranges in job postings in every country. Stripe strongly values pay transparency and is working toward pay transparency globally.

description:
About the job

Paid Ads Specialist (CPG Brands)

Part-Time | Remote | US Business Hours




About the Company




Our client is a fast-growing US-based CPG food brand known for its bold flavors, premium ingredients, and strong brand identity. The company has built a loyal customer base through a combination of high-quality products, creative marketing, and a focus on delivering exceptional consumer experiences.

They are looking for an experienced Paid Ads Specialist to lead and optimize paid media efforts across Meta and Google, driving customer acquisition, revenue growth, and profitable scaling.




About the Role

This role is ideal for a strategic and hands-on paid media professional with deep experience managing performance marketing campaigns for consumer packaged goods (CPG), food & beverage, or eCommerce brands.

You will own campaign strategy, execution, testing, optimization, and reporting across paid acquisition channels, working closely with leadership to scale revenue while maintaining strong efficiency metrics.




Key Responsibilities

Manage and optimize paid advertising campaigns across Meta and Google Ads

Develop customer acquisition strategies focused on profitable growth

Monitor and improve key performance metrics including ROAS, CAC, CPA, CTR, and conversion rate

Build and execute testing frameworks for creatives, audiences, landing pages, and offers

Analyze campaign performance and identify scaling opportunities

Manage advertising budgets and allocation across channels

Create performance reports and present insights and recommendations

Collaborate with creative and marketing teams to improve campaign results

Support seasonal promotions, product launches, and revenue-driving initiatives

Stay current on platform updates, industry trends, and best practices




Requirements

6+ years of hands-on experience managing Meta and/or Google Ads campaigns
Strong experience working with CPG, food & beverage, consumer brands, or eCommerce companies
Proven track record scaling paid media campaigns while maintaining profitability
Deep understanding of ROAS, CAC, CPA, MER, CTR, and customer acquisition economics
Experience managing significant monthly advertising budgets
Strong analytical and reporting skills
Ability to identify growth opportunities and make data-driven decisions
Excellent English communication skills
Ability to work independently in a remote environment




Nice to Have

Experience with DTC brands
Experience with Shopify
Familiarity with attribution and analytics tools such as GA4, Triple Whale, Northbeam, or similar platforms
Experience scaling seasonal and promotional campaigns

title: Community Manager
description:
About the job

Job Title: Community Manager




Job Type: Full-time




Location: Remote - Latin America




Job Summary:




Join our team as a dynamic Community Manager and play a pivotal role in creating a positive, collaborative, and engaged online environment. You will be at the forefront of customer support and community moderation, proactively resolving issues, answering questions, and ensuring a welcoming experience for all members. If you thrive in fast-paced remote settings and are eager to make a meaningful impact every day, this role is for you.




Key Responsibilities:




• Monitor and moderate online communities to ensure a safe, respectful, and inclusive atmosphere.

• Address customer complaints and questions promptly with empathy and professionalism.

• De-escalate conflicts and diffuse tense situations to maintain group harmony.

• Develop proactive engagement strategies to foster a vibrant and active community.

• Collaborate with internal teams to relay feedback and insights from the community.

• Manage time efficiently to handle multiple conversations and tasks in a remote environment.

• Create clear, friendly, and effective written and verbal communications across channels.




Required Skills and Qualifications:




• Proven experience in customer support and online community moderation.

• Exceptional verbal and written communication skills; a genuine care for clear and empathetic messaging.

• Strong conflict management and de-escalation abilities.

• Self-starter attitude with high energy and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

• Excellent time management skills in a remote work setting.

• Keen attention to detail and ability to multitask under pressure.

• Adaptable, with a willingness to go the extra mile to support community needs.




Preferred Qualifications:




• Background in managing large-scale online communities.

• Experience with various community platforms and CRM tools.

• Passion for building lasting relationships and driving positive user experiences.

description:
About the job

This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist (Part-Time) based in Mexico.

This is a hands-on, data-driven digital marketing role focused on driving measurable growth across paid media channels. You will be responsible for optimizing performance campaigns across Meta and Google Ads, turning insights into actionable improvements that directly impact lead generation and conversion outcomes. The role blends campaign execution, analytics, and experimentation, requiring a strong focus on ROI, attribution accuracy, and continuous optimization. You will also support tracking infrastructure using tools such as GA4 and Google Tag Manager to ensure clean, reliable marketing data. Working in a remote-first and collaborative environment, you will contribute to both strategic and tactical marketing decisions. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving performance puzzles, testing ideas quickly, and seeing clear business impact from their work. It offers the opportunity to work across global clients while shaping scalable digital growth strategies.

Accountabilities


Plan, manage, and optimize paid media campaigns across Meta Ads and Google Ads to drive qualified lead generation and improve ROI.
Execute ongoing campaign testing across audiences, creatives, bidding strategies, and landing pages to continuously improve performance.
Monitor and analyze campaign performance data, translating insights into actionable recommendations for optimization and growth.
Maintain accurate tracking and attribution systems using tools such as GA4, Google Tag Manager, Meta Pixel, and related conversion tracking platforms.
Ensure seamless flow of marketing data into CRM and marketing automation systems for accurate reporting and lead management.
Support SEO initiatives by contributing to on-page optimization and identifying opportunities for organic growth improvements.
Develop and deliver monthly performance reports, highlighting key metrics, insights, and optimization opportunities.
Identify trends, competitor movements, and emerging opportunities in digital marketing to inform campaign strategy.
Collaborate with stakeholders to improve messaging, creative direction, and conversion-focused strategies across campaigns.
Continuously optimize conversion rates and campaign efficiency across all digital channels.


Requirements


Minimum 2+ years of hands-on experience managing and optimizing paid media campaigns.
Strong expertise in Meta Ads Manager and Google Ads, with proven ability to drive performance outcomes.
Solid working knowledge of Google Tag Manager (GTM), Google Analytics 4 (GA4), Meta Pixel, and conversion tracking systems.
Experience working with CRM platforms, marketing automation tools, and lead generation workflows.
Strong understanding of performance marketing fundamentals including attribution, funnel optimization, and ROI measurement.
Ability to analyze campaign data and translate insights into clear, actionable recommendations.
Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with internal and external stakeholders.
Ability to work independently, manage priorities, and thrive in a remote, fast-paced environment.
Nice to have: exposure to SEO, landing page optimization, and multi-channel attribution models.


Benefits


Fully remote work setup with flexible working arrangements (part-time schedule).
Work-from-home support including company-provided equipment and connectivity assistance.
Exposure to international clients and global marketing environments.
Flexible work-life balance with a structured part-time schedule.
Opportunities for ongoing learning, professional development, and skill growth.
Collaborative, people-first culture that values autonomy and accountability.
Employee engagement activities, both virtual and in-person events.
Opportunity to directly impact campaign performance and measurable business growth.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

 Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Demand Generation Manager
skills: Diseñar, implementar y gestionar campañas pagas en plataformas como Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads, Meta (Facebook e Instagram), YouTube Ads, Spotify Ads, native ads (Outbrain o Taboola) y programmatic ads.
Optimización continua de campañas: Monitorear y ajustar las campañas para optimizar el rendimiento y el ROI.
Definir y aplicar estrategias de segmentación con audiencias personalizadas, audiencias similares (lookalike audiences), retargeting dinámico y señales de intención de compra (buyer intent signals).
Implementar estrategias de tracking avanzado y atribución multicanal para medir el impacto real de cada canal pagado en la conversión de leads.
Administrar y optimizar el presupuesto asignado a campañas, asegurando la máxima eficiencia en la generación de demanda.
Sincronizar campañas pagadas con HubSpot y otras plataformas de automatización de marketing para mejorar el lead nurturing y el retargeting dinámico.

Experiencia comprobable (mínimo 4 años) en gestión, ejecución y optimización de campañas de Demand Generation Manager, Paid Media, Performance Marketing Manager en entornos B2B, con foco en generación de demanda y leads calificados.
Experiencia hands-on operando campañas en múltiples plataformas digitales, incluyendo:
Google Ads
Meta Ads (Facebook e Instagram)
LinkedIn Ads
YouTube Ads
Native Ads (Outbrain, Taboola)
Programmatic Ads (DV360, Criteo, The Trade Desk)
Experiencia sólida en optimización basada en datos, trabajando con KPIs como CTR, CPC, CPL, CPA, ROAS y ROI, y tomando decisiones de inversión en tiempo real.
Experiencia en automatización y optimización avanzada, utilizando herramientas como:
Google Smart Bidding
Performance Max
Google Ads Scripts
Meta Automated Rules

title: E-commerce Simulation Expert (Remote)
skills: Role: E-commerce Simulation Expert
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: $40 - $50/hour
description:
About the job

Role: E-commerce Simulation Expert
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: $40 - $50/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking an E-commerce Simulation Expert to work on a contract basis. The role focuses on building and refining synthetic e-commerce environments that train and evaluate advanced AI models. Your expertise in real-world commerce will directly shape how AI systems learn, reason, and perform across critical domains. This work supports the development of reliable AI agents and contributes to the evolution of human-in-the-loop AI training.




Key Responsibilities:

• Design and construct synthetic digital stores with realistic product assortments, logical category structures, and authentic pricing models to reflect real-world e-commerce complexity.

• Generate, structure, and maintain comprehensive product catalogs ensuring high data quality, logical consistency, and alignment with market standards.

• Develop diverse shopping scenarios and user prompts that simulate varied consumer intents, preferences, and challenging edge cases to stress-test AI behavior.

• Create evaluation rubrics and conduct rigorous quality assurance to ensure generated data and simulations meet domain-specific accuracy and realism requirements.

• Collaborate with AI teams to refine prompt engineering and scenario design for continuous improvement of AI training datasets.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Demonstrated experience in e-commerce operations, including product data management, catalog structuring, or retail systems with a focus on data integrity and real-world relevance.

• Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills to design complex, logically consistent product hierarchies and pricing logic.

• Familiarity with AI/LLM prompting and evaluation principles, or ability to quickly learn how to structure inputs that generate high-quality training data for AI systems.

• Background in scenario design and user behavior modeling, preferably in retail, consumer goods, or related domains.

• Attention to detail and a quality assurance mindset to maintain high standards in synthetic data generation and simulation environments.

• Self-directed work style with ability to manage multiple asynchronous tasks and deliver consistent, high-quality output under defined deadlines.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers the chance to contribute directly to the development of frontier AI systems by leveraging real-world e-commerce expertise. You will work within a global network supporting AI research, with exposure to cutting-edge reinforcement learning environments and agent-based AI training. The position is ideal for professionals seeking meaningful impact through applied domain knowledge in a rapidly evolving field.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.




Apply Now!

title: CRO Manager
skills: Lead the development and execution of A/B tests, multivariate tests, and personalization strategies to improve conversion rates and optimize user experiences.
Conduct and analyze user research, heatmaps, session recordings, and surveys to identify drop-off points and pain points in the user journey.
Develop and implement a comprehensive CRO strategy focused on increasing conversions across all digital platforms, including web, mobile, and app interfaces.
Work closely with UX/UI designers, developers, and content teams to create and execute on optimization plans. Ensure changes are effectively implemented and tracked.
Use tools such as Google Analytics, Hotjar, and other CRO platforms to measure, analyze, and report on the effectiveness of experiments.
Apply a user-centered approach to solving design and usability challenges, creating smooth and effective user flows that drive desired outcomes.
Continuously refine the CRO process, advocating for best practices in testing and optimization within the organization.
Work closely with stakeholders from marketing, sales, and product teams in an agile environment, ensuring alignment on goals and strategies.
Minimum 3-4 years of experience in a CRO or digital optimization role, with a proven track record of improving conversion rates.
Strong understanding of A/B testing, multivariate testing, and personalization techniques. Expertise in using testing platforms (e.g., Optimizely, VWO) and analytical tools like Google Analytics.
Ability to draw actionable insights from user behavior data, and translate these insights into optimization plans.
Comfortable with the technical aspects of web analytics, basic HTML/CSS, and JavaScript, with the ability to communicate requirements to developers.
Understanding of UX/UI principles and how design impacts conversion rates.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present insights and recommendations to cross-functional teams.
High Ownership: Lead growth across a $100M+ brand platform
Creative Autonomy: Big sandbox, bold ideas encouraged
Cross-Brand Impact: Learn and scale across multiple industries and audiences
Fast Execution: We move quickly and prioritize performance
description:
About the job

About Pathfinder

Pathfinder is a brand platform on a mission to acquire, scale, and revitalize exceptional consumer brands in the U.S. Backed by a committed capital vehicle and led by founders with experience spanning Stanford GSB, Harvard Law, Y Combinator, Forbes 30U30, and deep expertise in e-commerce, marketing, and finance, we acquire high-potential DTC brands and unlock growth through hands-on operational excellence. We have acquired several brands to-date and are building a dream team of experts who want to turn previously mismanaged brands into breakout successes.

Our long-term vision: build a $100M+ portfolio of beloved, performance-driven consumer brands.




About the Role

We’re hiring an exceptional CRO Manager to turn traffic into purchases for 2 in-house DTC brands.

In this remote role, you will own and execute conversion optimization strategies to maximize website performance and marketing funnel efficiency. 




You’ll work closely with the Head of Growth, Design, and web development teams to create data-driven experiments, implement UX/UI improvements, and drive measurable conversion rate growth. 




What You’ll Do: 

Lead the development and execution of A/B tests, multivariate tests, and personalization strategies to improve conversion rates and optimize user experiences.
Conduct and analyze user research, heatmaps, session recordings, and surveys to identify drop-off points and pain points in the user journey.
Develop and implement a comprehensive CRO strategy focused on increasing conversions across all digital platforms, including web, mobile, and app interfaces.
Work closely with UX/UI designers, developers, and content teams to create and execute on optimization plans. Ensure changes are effectively implemented and tracked.
Use tools such as Google Analytics, Hotjar, and other CRO platforms to measure, analyze, and report on the effectiveness of experiments.
Apply a user-centered approach to solving design and usability challenges, creating smooth and effective user flows that drive desired outcomes.
Continuously refine the CRO process, advocating for best practices in testing and optimization within the organization.
Work closely with stakeholders from marketing, sales, and product teams in an agile environment, ensuring alignment on goals and strategies.




Skills, Knowledge, and Experience:
Minimum 3-4 years of experience in a CRO or digital optimization role, with a proven track record of improving conversion rates.
Strong understanding of A/B testing, multivariate testing, and personalization techniques. Expertise in using testing platforms (e.g., Optimizely, VWO) and analytical tools like Google Analytics.
Ability to draw actionable insights from user behavior data, and translate these insights into optimization plans.
Comfortable with the technical aspects of web analytics, basic HTML/CSS, and JavaScript, with the ability to communicate requirements to developers.
Understanding of UX/UI principles and how design impacts conversion rates.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present insights and recommendations to cross-functional teams.




Why Join Pathfinder?

High Ownership: Lead growth across a $100M+ brand platform
Creative Autonomy: Big sandbox, bold ideas encouraged
Cross-Brand Impact: Learn and scale across multiple industries and audiences
Fast Execution: We move quickly and prioritize performance

description:
About the job

About Klar

Let’s start with the basics! Klar is a Mexican fintech whose mission is to democratize and revolutionize how financial services are delivered in Mexico — especially since half of the population still doesn’t have a bank account. Since our launch in 2019, we’ve served more than 7 million users with a growing suite of debit and credit products, and the future is brighter than ever.

Today, we’re not only scaling our consumer business, but also building Klar Empresas (B2B) from the ground up — a new division focused on empowering Mexican SMEs with the financial tools, capital, and services they need to grow. This is an exciting new chapter where we get to design and build from scratch while leveraging the trust and scale of Klar’s platform.

We are also proud to have been certified as a “Great Place to Work” in Mexico — something we’ve worked hard to achieve and will continue to build on.




Our people

If you join us at Klar, you’ll be welcomed to a team which is rich in many talents and we are very proud! With our head office in Mexico City, and remote tech hubs in Berlin and Argentina, we are always learning something new about another culture or language. With so many people from different backgrounds and walks of life (young professionals, parents, LGBTQ+, neurodivergence), you’ll definitely find your people here!




Our values

Ownership - We own our successes & our failures as a team.
Excellence - We do everything to the best of our ability & always seek to achieve a new level of excellence in our work.
Inclusion - We believe we are stronger together and actively work to promote a safe, diverse, inclusive, and respectful culture.
Customer Obsession - We understand the value Klar can bring to its customers & it’s always at the forefront of our decisions.
Klarity - We communicate clearly & with authenticity. It’s in our name & it’s what we do.




The position and your daily adventures

We’re looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist to execute, optimize, and scale paid acquisition campaigns across Google, Meta, and other performance platforms. This is a hands-on role for someone who loves data, experimentation, and turning budgets into measurable growth.

You will own the day-to-day operation of performance campaigns, continuously improving CAC, ROAS, and funnel conversion, working closely with creative, analytics, and growth teams. You’ll be part of a high-performance team managing a US$2M+ monthly budget.







What you can expect:

Reporting: Create weekly and ad-hoc performance reports. Make financial analysis of campaign spend.
Own campaign tracking, attribution setup, and pixel/event health.
Operate and optimize paid campaigns: Constant monitoring and daily adjustments of paid ads campaigns on Google, Facebook and new channels.
Launch new campaigns, ad sets, creatives, and targeting strategies
Monitor performance daily and implement data-driven optimizations.
Run A/B tests on creatives, audiences, landing pages, and funnels.
Collaborate with creative and product teams to improve conversion.
KEY KPIs: New users and approved credit lines, Cost per Acquisition (CpA), and ROAS.




What we are looking for:

Bachelor's degree in Industrial Engineering, Business Engineering, Digital Transformation Engineering, (or similar) with an orientation to data analysis.
2+ years hands-on experience running performance campaigns on: Meta and Google.
Strong analytical skills, comfort working with performance dashboards and experience with SQL.
Experience with conversion tracking, pixels, and attribution tools
Ability to do Data-driven decision-making
High ownership, attention to detail, and execution speed
Advanced English level
Experience in fintech, apps, or subscription products is a plus




Our offer to you:

Competitive salary based on performance and experience
Chance of earning Klar stock options
15 days of paid vacation per year; plus extended maternity and paternity leaves
Vacation premium
Food vouchers
30 days of Christmas bonus
Medical Insurance
Computer devices

Sponsored coaching and therapy sessions
A modern centrally located office in Mexico City with free drinks, snacks, and regular social events
International work environment with amazing and highly skilled people
A world-class team that helps you evolve your skills in areas you're interested in




Klar is a safe place for everyone!

We trust our highly skilled and diverse team, and we’re committed to creating a welcoming and inclusive environment for new talents to flourish. We value diversity and welcome all applications regardless of gender, nationality, ethnic and social origin, religion/belief, physical abilities, age, sexual orientation and identity.

Should you require any accommodations through the recruitment process, please don’t hesitate to let us know how we can help!

description:
About the job

Overview

CAREERS TO SMILE ABOUT 

At PepsiCo, you’ll discover a place where our mission is to create smiles around the world. With a portfolio of more than 500 beloved brands including, Gatorade, Lay’s and Quaker, our work touches millions of people every day.

At the heart of the company is a team of thinkers, creators, and problem-solvers who collaborate to innovate and turn ideas into action. Driven by innovation and a focus on creating joyful moments through food and drinks, our decisions are guided by consumer centricity, creating opportunities for our associates to do meaningful work and make a lasting impact in the communities we serve.

Whatever your role, you’ll be part of a global community that values your ideas and empowers you to make an impact, on your career and on the world around you.

Responsibilities

The Opportunity 

As a One Touch & Process Automation Analyst, the candidate will gain in-depth exposure to end-to-end purchasing operations for beverage products while supporting the U.S. beverage market. The position offers a unique opportunity to contribute to key operational processes, including invoice validation and Proof of Delivery (POD) reviews, while driving impactful process improvement initiatives.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone passionate about automation, data analysis, and continuous improvement. The selected candidate will play a key role in identifying inefficiencies, developing automation solutions, and enhancing daily operational processes that directly support business performance.

Your Impact 


Develop and implement automation solutions to improve purchasing processes and operational efficiency.
Manage inventory records and maintain purchasing and inventory reports to support business decisions.
Review invoices and Proof of Delivery (POD) documentation to ensure accuracy and compliance.
Investigate and resolve delivery discrepancies by coordinating with carriers and Customer Care teams.
Identify opportunities for process improvement and support continuous improvement initiatives across purchasing operations.


Qualifications

Who Are We Looking For? 


Experience in process improvement and operational efficiency initiatives.
Fluent English (written and spoken).
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint.
Knowledge of Microsoft Power Platform (Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power Pages).
Ability to learn and navigate multiple systems and platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Proactive, curious, and self-driven mindset.
Excellent teamwork and communication skills.
Knowledge of programming languages or scripting tools is a plus, but not required.


If this is an opportunity that interests you, we encourage you to apply even if you do not meet 100% of the requirements.

What can you expect from us: 


Opportunities to learn and develop every day through a wide range of programs. 
Internal digital platforms that promote self-learning. 
Development programs according to Leadership skills. 
Specialized training according to the role. 
Learning experiences with internal and external providers. 
We love to celebrate success, which is why we have recognition programs for seniority, behavior, leadership, moments of life, among others. 
Financial wellness programs that will help you reach your goals in all stages of life. 
A flexibility program that will allow you to balance your personal and work life, adapting your working day to your lifestyle. 
And because your family is also important to us, they can also enjoy benefits such as our Wellness Line, thousands of Agreements and Discounts, Scholarship programs for your children, Aid Plans for different moments of life, among others. 


We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We respect and value diversity as a work force and innovation for the organization.

title: Specialist, Content Optimization
skills: Experience with tools like Helium 10, Data Dive, or Jungle Scout.
Familiarity with Amazon's A+ Content and Brand Store modules.

description:
About the job

Brainlabs is the media agency built to answer one question: what's actually driving profit? Founded in 2012 by Daniel Gilbert, we were built by engineers before we were a media agency. Today, 1,000+ Brainlabbers across five continents use our proprietary agents, built on 32 media tools and over 2,500 logged experiments, to help brands connect every channel they plan and buy to one thing: the bottom line.

The role

Brands live and die by their product pages. A great product with poor content gets buried. A well-optimized listing with clear titles, strong keywords, and accurate attributes climbs. This role

owns that gap.

As a Content Optimization and Catalogue Management Specialist, you'll be responsible for how our clients products show up, get found, and convert on Amazon and Mercado Libre. That means writing and refining listings, managing catalogue health, and building the kind of organic visibility that doesn't disappear the moment ad spend drops.

You'll work directly with client teams and internal strategists to keep catalogues clean, content sharp, and rankings moving in the right direction.

What will you do?

Your day-to-day breaks into two connected areas.

Content Optimization

Content optimization is where you drive organic performance. You'll conduct keyword research, write and update product titles, bullet points, descriptions, and backend attributes. You'll audit existing content against platform best practices and identify gaps before they become ranking problems. You'll test copy variations and track what actually moves the needle.

Catalogue Management

Catalogue management is where you keep the operation running. You'll handle the publication and maintenance of product listings, manage bulk uploads and flat files, resolve listing errors

and suppressed ASINs or items, and keep content synchronized across multiple accounts and categories. When something breaks, you figure out why and fix it.

Beyond those two pillars, you'll monitor organic ranking performance, report on content health metrics, and bring recommendations that are grounded in data, not guesswork.

What are we looking for?

Experience

Around three years working in content optimization and/or catalogue management on Amazon and/or Mercado Libre. You understand how these platforms rank organic results and you've

influenced those rankings for real brands.

Platform Fluency

You know Seller Central and Vendor Central. You've worked with Mercado Libre item publication flows. Flat files, bulk uploads, listing errors, and suppressed items are familiar

territory, not new concepts.

Keyword and SEO Fundamentals

You know how to research, prioritize, and place keywords in a way that platforms reward. You understand the difference between what drives impressions and what drives conversions.

Analytical Mindset

You read performance data and draw conclusions from it. If organic rankings drop, you have a process for diagnosing why. If a content change works, you can explain what it did and why it helped.

Attention to Detail

Catalogue work is unforgiving. A wrong attribute, a missing image, a misformatted file: these things cause real problems. You catch errors before they ship.

English Proficiency

You're comfortable reading, writing, and communicating professionally in English. You'll work with international teams and documentation.

Nice to Have


Experience with tools like Helium 10, Data Dive, or Jungle Scout.
Familiarity with Amazon's A+ Content and Brand Store modules.
Experience across multiple categories or markets.



What happens next?

We know searching for a job is tough and that you want to find the best career and employer for you. We also want to ensure that this position is the best fit for both you and us. Therefore, you will participate in a comprehensive interview process that includes skills interviews with our team. The goal of this process is to allow you to get to know us as we learn more about you.

Brainlabs actively seeks and encourages applications from candidates with diverse backgrounds and identities. We are proud to be an equal opportunity workplace: we are committed to equal opportunity for all applicants and employees regardless of age, disability, sex, gender reassignment, sexual orientation, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion, or belief, and marriage and civil partnerships. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation during the application process, please let us know!

Please note that we will never ask you to transfer cash or make any other payment to us in order to apply for a role or to work for Brainlabs. Any such asks are fraudulent and should be reported to the appropriate authorities in your area.

description:
About the job

About Charly Agency

 Charly Agency is a growing digital agency specializing in web design, UX/UI, branding, ecommerce, and development for clients primarily in the US. Operating globally with offices in San Francisco and Barcelona, we partner with brands ranging from startups to enterprises. Our team is spread across Europe and Latin America and we are scaling fast.




About the Role

 We are looking for a freelance copywriter to join our pool of creative collaborators. You will write across brand, web, and marketing, working closely with our design and strategy team to bring client projects to life through sharp, purposeful copy.

This is not a beginner role. We want someone who can take a brief, understand the business behind it, and produce copy that is clear, purposeful, and built to convert — without hand-holding. Mistakes happen, but high quality, great communication, and honesty are non-negotiable. No vague communication, no hiding behind "I was waiting for feedback."




What You'll Do

Write website copy across homepages, landing pages, product pages, and about pages
Develop brand voice guidelines and messaging frameworks
Write case studies, blog posts, and marketing content
Collaborate with designers to ensure copy and design work together




What We're Looking For

Strong portfolio of web and brand copywriting
Excellent written English, this is non-negotiable
Experience writing for DTC, SaaS, B2B, or CPG brands is a plus
Comfortable delivering quickly under tight deadlines without sacrificing quality
Clear and thoughtful approach to using AI in your workflow
Reliable, communicative, and comfortable working async
Eager to learn, open to direction, and quick on feedback
Agency experience is a strong plus




About Us

 Charly is a creative agency with a warm, international team that takes the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously. We collaborate across time zones, support each other, and care a lot about doing great work for clients we genuinely like. If you are kind, talented, and ambitious, you will fit right in.




To Apply, send:

Your strongest copywriting samples (website, landing page, or brand copy preferred)
Examples of projects you wrote for and your role in them
Your availability and preferred working hours
Your expected rate (hourly or per project)
