description:
About the job

¡Ayúdanos a impulsar el futuro de la tecnología en salud!

En Top Doctors Group trabajamos para mejorar la forma en la que las personas acceden a la atención médica. A través de la tecnología, conectamos pacientes, profesionales sanitarios y clínicas para ofrecer una experiencia más accesible, eficiente y humana.

Hoy estamos presentes en 10 países y desarrollamos soluciones digitales que ayudan a miles de profesionales sanitarios a potenciar su actividad y a millones de personas a encontrar la atención sanitaria que necesitan.

Creemos que la tecnología genera un mayor impacto cuando se pone al servicio de las personas. Por eso seguimos creciendo y buscando talento que quiera ayudarnos a construir el futuro de la salud digital en un entorno internacional, innovador y en constante evolución.

💡 ¿Por qué este rol es interesante?

Porque tendrás un impacto directo en el crecimiento de Top Doctors en Latinoamérica. Como Performance Marketing Specialist, liderarás la estrategia de captación digital de pacientes, médicos y centros médicos, siendo responsable de generar oportunidades de negocio a través de campañas de paid media orientadas a resultados.

Tendrás ownership sobre todo el ciclo de las campañas: desde la definición de la estrategia y la gestión de presupuestos hasta la optimización continua y el análisis de resultados. Trabajarás de forma muy cercana con los equipos de Ventas, Marketing y Revenue para asegurar que los leads generados se conviertan en crecimiento real para el negocio.

Tu trabajo tendrá una influencia directa en métricas clave como la generación de leads, el coste de adquisición, la calidad del pipeline comercial y el retorno de la inversión publicitaria, contribuyendo al crecimiento de una de las compañías HealthTech líderes en Latinoamérica.

🚀 Lo que harás en tu día a día


Planear, implementar y optimizar campañas de paid media en Google Ads (Search, Display, YouTube, Performance Max), Meta Ads (Facebook e Instagram) y redes de afiliados.
Liderar la captación digital de los tres segmentos clave: pacientes, médicos/especialistas y centros médicos o clínicas.
Gestionar y distribuir el presupuesto de inversión publicitaria (media budget) para maximizar el ROI, minimizar el CPA/CAC y escalar la adquisición de forma sostenible.
Dar seguimiento continuo a los leads generados y colaborar con el equipo de Ventas para cerrar el loop de conversión, identificando fricciones y oportunidades de mejora.
Definir, monitorear y reportar los KPIs de performance: CPL, CPC, CPA, ROAS, CAC, tasa de conversión, LTV y revenue atribuido a campañas.
Configurar, mantener y auditar el tracking de conversiones mediante Google Tag Manager, pixels de Meta, eventos de GA4 y herramientas de CRM.
Ejecutar pruebas A/B estructuradas de creatividades, audiencias, segmentaciones y landing pages para mejorar sistemáticamente el rendimiento de las campañas.
Explorar y activar nuevos canales de captación: programmatic advertising, native ads, TikTok Ads, LinkedIn Ads u otros que representen oportunidades para el negocio.
Elaborar reportes periódicos de performance con análisis de resultados, insights accionables y recomendaciones de optimización para la dirección de Marketing.
Colaborar con los equipos de Contenido y Diseño para briefear y validar los activos creativos necesarios para las campañas (banners, videos, landing pages, copies).


👀 Nos encantará conocerte si…

Si Disfrutas Tomando Decisiones Basadas En Datos, Optimizando Campañas Con Impacto Real En El Negocio y Trabajando En Entornos Donde Marketing y Revenue Están Estrechamente Conectados, Probablemente Encajarás Muy Bien En Este Rol Si Tienes

✔ Entre 3 y 5 años de experiencia en Performance Marketing, Paid Media o Growth Marketing.

✔ Experiencia gestionando campañas de adquisición en Google Ads y Meta Ads de forma autónoma.

✔ Experiencia trabajando con objetivos de negocio vinculados a generación de leads, adquisición de clientes y revenue.

✔ Capacidad para gestionar presupuestos publicitarios y optimizar inversiones en función del ROI.

✔ Dominio avanzado de Google Ads Manager y Meta Ads Manager.

✔ Experiencia avanzada con Google Analytics 4 (GA4) y Google Tag Manager (GTM).

✔ Conocimiento de herramientas de reporting y visualización de datos como Looker Studio, Power BI o similares.

✔ Experiencia utilizando CRM como Salesforce, HubSpot o equivalentes para el seguimiento del ciclo de vida del lead.

✔ Buen manejo de Excel o Google Sheets para análisis de datos y modelización de métricas.

✔ Mentalidad analítica, orientación a resultados y capacidad para convertir datos en acciones concretas.

✔ Español nativo o nivel avanzado e inglés intermedio (B1+) para trabajar con documentación técnica y colaborar con equipos internacionales.

➕ Será un plus contar con certificaciones de Google Ads o Meta Blueprint, experiencia en entornos HealthTech, SaaS o plataformas digitales, y conocimientos de herramientas de CRO o testing como Unbounce o VWO.

🎯 Cómo nos imaginamos a la persona que tendrá éxito en este rol

Más Allá De La Experiencia Técnica, Creemos Que Las Personas Que Mejor Encajan En Este Tipo De Posición Suelen Compartir Algunas Formas De Trabajar

✔ Toman decisiones basadas en datos y entienden cómo sus acciones impactan en métricas de negocio como el ROI, la rentabilidad y el crecimiento.

✔ Gestionan presupuestos de inversión con criterio, buscando siempre maximizar el impacto de cada euro invertido.

✔ Disfrutan analizando resultados, identificando oportunidades de mejora y optimizando continuamente campañas, procesos y estrategias.

✔ Entienden que el éxito del marketing no termina con la generación de leads, sino que colaboran activamente con los equipos comerciales para mejorar la conversión y la calidad de las oportunidades generadas.

✔ Prestan atención al detalle en aspectos técnicos como el tracking, la medición y la calidad de los datos, asegurando que las decisiones se basen en información fiable.

✔ Se sienten cómodas trabajando en entornos dinámicos, adaptándose con rapidez a los cambios de mercado, nuevas plataformas, algoritmos y comportamientos de los usuarios.

💙 Qué encontrarás al unirte a Top Doctors Group


📈 Posibilidades reales de crecimiento y desarrollo profesional.
💻 Modalidad de trabajo presencial.
🕑 Jornada completa de lunes a viernes de 9:00h a 18:00h
🌎 Un entorno internacional, dinámico y colaborativo
👥 Líderes que priorizan el acompañamiento, la autonomía y el desarrollo de las personas
🥳 Un equipo con el que aprender, crecer y disfrutar del camino


🌱 Diversidad e inclusión

En Top Doctors Group creemos que la diversidad de experiencias, perspectivas y trayectorias nos ayuda a construir mejores equipos y mejores soluciones. Trabajamos para construir un entorno diverso, inclusivo y donde todas las personas tengan las mismas oportunidades para desarrollarse profesionalmente. Si necesitas cualquier adaptación o apoyo durante el proceso de selección, estaremos encantados de ayudarte.

🚀 ¿Te interesa?

Si has llegado hasta aquí y te identificas en buena parte con lo que buscamos, nos encantará conocerte. No creemos en las candidaturas perfectas ni en los perfiles que encajan al 100% sobre el papel, más allá de la experiencia concreta, valoramos aquellas competencias, habilidades y formas de trabajar que permiten aportar valor al equipo y contribuir al crecimiento de la compañía. Si crees que este reto puede encajar contigo, ¡aplica ahora!

Fundada en 2013, Top Doctors Group es un grupo de empresas tecnológicas líder en la digitalización del sector sanitario. A lo largo de nuestra trayectoria, hemos incorporado empresas especializadas y ampliado nuestro modelo hacia soluciones B2B, consolidándonos como un referente internacional en salud digital.

Nuestro propósito es claro: ayudar a cada persona a encontrar al mejor especialista cuando más lo necesita. Hoy, más de 1 millón de personas consultan nuestras plataformas cada mes y más de 10 millones de pacientes han confiado en nosotros en Europa, LATAM, Estados Unidos y Oriente Medio.

A través de nuestras marcas —Top Doctors, Top Health Tech, Ofimedic, iWantGreatCare, Manitex y Top Farma— impulsamos una atención médica más innovadora, eficiente y humana. Somos una compañía global en crecimiento continuo, con un propósito firme: transformar el futuro de la salud a través de la tecnología, el talento y la innovación.

description:
About the job
This job is sourced from a job board. Learn More

Work From Anywhere in LATAM and the Philippines

Work Schedule: Full-time, Monday–Friday | Significant daily overlap with US business hours (ET/PT)

Compensation Range: $1,500 - $2,000 USD/month based on experience

Our client is a lean, high-trust paid media agency that manages Google Ads across a portfolio of home improvement contractor accounts. They're hiring a Google Ads Specialist to build, launch, optimize, and QA campaigns with precision — turning founder-led strategy into flawless execution.

The team is small and founder-led: strategy is owned at the top, and execution quality is everything. At this spend level, every campaign build matters, and the work is treated with the rigor that high-stakes accounts demand. This is an execution role, not a strategy role.

Day to day, you'll own end-to-end campaign setup across assigned accounts — targeting, geos, budgets, bids, ad copy, and extensions — while implementing and QA-ing conversion tracking across Google Ads and GA4. You'll monitor accounts daily, run A/B tests, flag pacing and performance issues before you're asked, and pull clean reports in Looker Studio. Success means campaigns that go live error-free and perform against CPA and ROAS targets.

Requirements

Must-Have


3–5 years of hands-on Google Ads experience, ideally agency-side managing multiple client accounts simultaneously
Proficiency in the Google Ads UI and Google Ads Editor to build campaigns end-to-end (targeting, geos, budgets, bids, ad copy, extensions)
Hands-on experience implementing and QA-ing conversion tracking and attribution across Google Ads and GA4
Ability to QA full campaign builds pre-launch — verifying geo targeting, URLs, tracking, budgets, and bid settings before anything goes live
Reporting fluency in Looker Studio (or equivalent), including dashboard setup and clear analysis of account trends and anomalies
Working command of paid media metrics — CPA, ROAS, conversion rate, and Quality Score — to inform optimization decisions
English proficiency at C1 (CEFR) for concise written and verbal reporting to a founder


Nice-to-Have


Google Tag Manager experience (tag configuration and troubleshooting)
GA4 advanced setup, Conversion API, or server-side tracking exposure
Home improvement, contractor, or local services vertical experience
Active Google Ads certifications


About Pearl Talent

Pearl works with the top 1% of candidates from around the world and connects them with the best startups in the US and EU. Our clients have raised over $5B in aggregate and are backed by companies like OpenAI, a16z, and Founders Fund. They're looking for the sharpest, hungriest candidates who they can consistently promote and work with over many years. Candidates we've hired have been flown out to the US and EU to work with their clients, and even promoted to roles that match folks onshore in the US.

Hear why we exist, what we believe in, and who we're building for:

Watch here: https://www.loom.com/share/863b8e426e6d48309aa8f4ea4c1c94cd

description:
About the job

This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist (Part-Time) based in Mexico.

This is a hands-on, data-driven digital marketing role focused on driving measurable growth across paid media channels. You will be responsible for optimizing performance campaigns across Meta and Google Ads, turning insights into actionable improvements that directly impact lead generation and conversion outcomes. The role blends campaign execution, analytics, and experimentation, requiring a strong focus on ROI, attribution accuracy, and continuous optimization. You will also support tracking infrastructure using tools such as GA4 and Google Tag Manager to ensure clean, reliable marketing data. Working in a remote-first and collaborative environment, you will contribute to both strategic and tactical marketing decisions. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys solving performance puzzles, testing ideas quickly, and seeing clear business impact from their work. It offers the opportunity to work across global clients while shaping scalable digital growth strategies.

Accountabilities


Plan, manage, and optimize paid media campaigns across Meta Ads and Google Ads to drive qualified lead generation and improve ROI.
Execute ongoing campaign testing across audiences, creatives, bidding strategies, and landing pages to continuously improve performance.
Monitor and analyze campaign performance data, translating insights into actionable recommendations for optimization and growth.
Maintain accurate tracking and attribution systems using tools such as GA4, Google Tag Manager, Meta Pixel, and related conversion tracking platforms.
Ensure seamless flow of marketing data into CRM and marketing automation systems for accurate reporting and lead management.
Support SEO initiatives by contributing to on-page optimization and identifying opportunities for organic growth improvements.
Develop and deliver monthly performance reports, highlighting key metrics, insights, and optimization opportunities.
Identify trends, competitor movements, and emerging opportunities in digital marketing to inform campaign strategy.
Collaborate with stakeholders to improve messaging, creative direction, and conversion-focused strategies across campaigns.
Continuously optimize conversion rates and campaign efficiency across all digital channels.


Requirements


Minimum 2+ years of hands-on experience managing and optimizing paid media campaigns.
Strong expertise in Meta Ads Manager and Google Ads, with proven ability to drive performance outcomes.
Solid working knowledge of Google Tag Manager (GTM), Google Analytics 4 (GA4), Meta Pixel, and conversion tracking systems.
Experience working with CRM platforms, marketing automation tools, and lead generation workflows.
Strong understanding of performance marketing fundamentals including attribution, funnel optimization, and ROI measurement.
Ability to analyze campaign data and translate insights into clear, actionable recommendations.
Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with internal and external stakeholders.
Ability to work independently, manage priorities, and thrive in a remote, fast-paced environment.
Nice to have: exposure to SEO, landing page optimization, and multi-channel attribution models.


Benefits


Fully remote work setup with flexible working arrangements (part-time schedule).
Work-from-home support including company-provided equipment and connectivity assistance.
Exposure to international clients and global marketing environments.
Flexible work-life balance with a structured part-time schedule.
Opportunities for ongoing learning, professional development, and skill growth.
Collaborative, people-first culture that values autonomy and accountability.
Employee engagement activities, both virtual and in-person events.
Opportunity to directly impact campaign performance and measurable business growth.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

 Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses and identifying potential inconsistencies or verification signals in application materials based on available information. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

description:
About the job

El Puerto de Liverpool es la empresa líder en tiendas departamentales en México. CON MÁS DE 170 AÑOS EN EL MERCADO, nos dedicamos a facilitar la selección de productos y brindarles el mejor servicio a las familias mexicanas, esperando superar las expectativas de calidad, moda, valor y atención dentro de un ambiente agradable.

Contamos con cinco unidades de negocio: Tiendas Liverpool, Tiendas Suburbia, Centros Comerciales Galerías, Boutiques y Negocios Financieros, componiendo una empresa sólida, que ofrece a sus colaboradores estabilidad financiera y el orgullo de pertenecer a una EMPRESA 100% MEXICANA.

NUESTRA MISIÓN ES SERVIR AL CLIENTE EN TODO LUGAR, TODOS LOS DÍAS, TODA LA VIDA, nuestra visión es ser la mejor opción en servicio, surtido y valor. Nuestros valores nos guían hacia el trabajo en equipo, productividad, innovación e integridad.

Misión Del Cargo

Apoyar y liderar la estrategia de Brand Marketing junto a la Gerencia, asegurando planeación, ejecución y seguimiento.

Gestionar campañas de temporada, lanzamientos y colaboraciones, coordinando planes de marketing (GTM), briefs y agencias creativas, medios, PR e influencers, con objetivos claros por etapa del funnel y siempre dentro del presupuesto de inversión (A&P budget).
Colaborar con equipos de Wholesale, Retail, Digital, Trade, VM y PR para asegurar que las campañas se adapten a los distintos canales y necesidades de la marca, garantizando posicionamiento adecuado y apego al presupuesto de inversión.
Analizar, dar seguimiento y gestionar administrativamente el desempeño de marketing mediante KPI’s, reportes post-campaña y control de presupuesto, asegurando procesos eficientes con agencias y proveedores, cumplimiento de tiempos y entregables definidos en los planes de marketing (GTM).

title: Marketing Specialist
skills: Digital Marketing Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies aimed at first responder agencies and healthcare professionals.
Content Creation: Craft compelling content including blog posts, newsletters, and social media updates that resonate with our target audience.
SEO Management: Optimize website content for search engines to enhance visibility for relevant keywords related to mental health services for first responders.
Social Media Engagement: Manage social media platforms, creating engaging posts that drive awareness and interaction among followers.
Analytics and Reporting: Analyze marketing performance metrics to assess the effectiveness of campaigns and suggest improvements.
Audience Segmentation: Identify and segment target audiences to tailor messaging specifically for different groups, including agencies and individual first responders.
Networking and Outreach: Develop relationships with potential referral sources, including healthcare providers and first responder agencies, to expand our reach.
Collaboration: Work closely with our team to align marketing efforts with overall practice goals and client needs.
Experience: Minimum 2+ years of experience marketing for American-based companies (Healthcare or Service Industry preferred).
Technical Savvy: Current with modern marketing technologies and social media algorithms; ability to use AI tools to optimize workflow (but not rely on them for core creativity).
Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English skills. While primarily a creative role, you must be comfortable making occasional outbound "warm" contacts to build referral relationships.
Mindset: A "shark" mentality—proactive, aggressive in pursuing visibility, and capable of working without constant micromanagement.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is highly preferred.
Monitoring Tools: Open to using monitoring tools like Hubstaff.
description:
About the job

✨ IMPORTANT – WATCH THIS QUICK LOOM VIDEO ON HOW TO GET HIRED:

📽️ https://www.loom.com/share/345f776084e642ba99c24aabe40a67a0

🇬🇧 C1 ENGLISH LEVEL IS REQUIRED.

The selected candidate will be speaking strictly in English during their entire shift.

🎤 PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND AUDIO RECORDING IN ENGLISH




Job Details




JOB TITLE

Marketing Specialist

JOB ID

BYRTIA1

INDUSTRY

Medical

LOCATION

LATAM / South Africa / PH if needed

JOB STATUS

Part Time (to full time)

WORK SCHEDULE

Monday to Friday, availability during EST business hours

SALARY

$8 - $11 per hour

TARGET START DATE

ASAP




ROLE OVERVIEW




About the Client:

The Client is a growing psychological therapy and testing practice. We specialize in providing high-quality therapy and diagnostic testing (e.g., for first responders and police candidates). We are currently at a pivotal growth phase and are looking for a proactive, tech-savvy marketing professional to help us overcome our current financial bottleneck and scale our team of clinicians.




About the Role:

Our Client is seeking a high-energy Marketing Specialist (Virtual Assistant). This is not a passive task-taking role; they need a strategic thinker who understands the American healthcare landscape and can proactively drive lead generation. You will be responsible for translating the clinical expertise of the Client into a digital presence that attracts the "right fit" clients—specifically those seeking therapy and testing rather than medication management.




Key Responsibilities

Digital Marketing Strategy: Develop and execute comprehensive marketing strategies aimed at first responder agencies and healthcare professionals.
Content Creation: Craft compelling content including blog posts, newsletters, and social media updates that resonate with our target audience.
SEO Management: Optimize website content for search engines to enhance visibility for relevant keywords related to mental health services for first responders.
Social Media Engagement: Manage social media platforms, creating engaging posts that drive awareness and interaction among followers.
Analytics and Reporting: Analyze marketing performance metrics to assess the effectiveness of campaigns and suggest improvements.
Audience Segmentation: Identify and segment target audiences to tailor messaging specifically for different groups, including agencies and individual first responders.
Networking and Outreach: Develop relationships with potential referral sources, including healthcare providers and first responder agencies, to expand our reach.
Collaboration: Work closely with our team to align marketing efforts with overall practice goals and client needs.




Qualifications & Skills

Experience: Minimum 2+ years of experience marketing for American-based companies (Healthcare or Service Industry preferred).
Technical Savvy: Current with modern marketing technologies and social media algorithms; ability to use AI tools to optimize workflow (but not rely on them for core creativity).
Communication: Exceptional written and verbal English skills. While primarily a creative role, you must be comfortable making occasional outbound "warm" contacts to build referral relationships.
Mindset: A "shark" mentality—proactive, aggressive in pursuing visibility, and capable of working without constant micromanagement.
Education: Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field is highly preferred.
Monitoring Tools: Open to using monitoring tools like Hubstaff.

description:
About the job

Acerca de nosotros




Somos una agencia global, especialista en medios, marketing y tecnología. Líderes en el ramo con presencia en más de 100 países. Creamos soluciones integrales para todos nuestros clientes de forma rápida y eficaz.




Nuestra búsqueda constante de mejorar en lo que hacemos comienza contactando con personas como tú.




OMG te invita a unirte a nuestro equipo como: Ejecutivo de medios offline




¿Qué buscamos?




Buscamos un perfil con experiencia en la ejecución y seguimiento de campañas de medios tradicionales dentro de agencias de medios o centrales de medios, con interés en desarrollarse en un entorno dinámico y colaborativo.




Licenciatura en Mercadotecnia, Publicidad, Comunicación, Administración o afines.
Experiencia mínima de 2 años en implementación, ejecución y seguimiento de campañas offline.
Experiencia indispensable en agencias de medios o centrales de medios.
Conocimiento de medios tradicionales como TV, Radio, OOH, Prensa y Revistas.
Inglés intermedio.
Manejo de Excel y herramientas de seguimiento y control de campañas.




¿Cuáles son las principales actividades?




Apoyar en la elaboración de planes tácticos mediante análisis de audiencias, competencia y tendencias de mercado.
Ejecutar la compra de medios tradicionales (TV, Radio, OOH, Prensa y Revistas) de acuerdo con los planes aprobados por el cliente.
Gestionar la generación y seguimiento de órdenes de compra en los sistemas internos de la compañía.
Coordinar el envío de materiales creativos y dar seguimiento con medios y proveedores para la correcta implementación de campañas.
Solicitar, recopilar y validar testigos para asegurar la correcta transmisión o publicación de las campañas.
Monitorear campañas activas y dar seguimiento a incidencias durante su ejecución.
Realizar conciliaciones de campañas verificando que la ejecución coincida con lo planeado y contratado.
Gestionar el cierre administrativo de campañas y órdenes de compra para el proceso de facturación.
Elaborar reportes post-buy y análisis de resultados de campaña.




¿Qué ofrecemos?




Un entorno laboral positivo y colaborativo, basado en el respeto y el trabajo en equipo.
Excelentes beneficios que promueven tu bienestar personal y tu desarrollo profesional.
Prestaciones superiores a las de la ley.
Un espacio seguro y libre de discriminación, donde cada voz es valorada y cada persona realmente importa.




Si estás buscando más que solo un empleo y quieres formar parte de una comunidad que te inspira y te impulsa a alcanzar tu máximo potencial, ¡nos encantaría conocerte!




En OMG NO discriminamos por razón de raza, origen étnico, edad, sexo, género, estado civil, ideología, opiniones políticas, nacionalidad, religión, orientación sexual, identidad o expresión de género o cualquier otra condición personal, física o social. A través de ello, construimos una cultura incluyente, de respeto y diversa.

title: Growth & Strategy Specialist - México
skills: Diseñar y liderar la estrategia de go-to-market para México: segmentación del mercado de colegios privados, priorización geográfica y definición de canales de adquisición.
Definir y ser owner de las métricas clave: nuevos colegios adquiridos, valor de pipeline, CAC, tasa de conversión y penetración en el segmento premium del mercado.
Construir relaciones y alianzas con redes de colegios privados, asociaciones educativas e instituciones clave para acelerar la adquisición.
Diseñar y ejecutar experimentos de crecimiento con metodología rigurosa: hipótesis claras, medición de resultados y escalado de lo que funciona.
Coordinar con Marketing la generación de demanda inbound y las estrategias de activación y nurturing del pipeline.
Optimizar el funnel de adquisición de extremo a extremo, desde el primer contacto hasta el cierre y el handoff a Customer Success.
Construir, liderar y desarrollar el equipo de Growth en México, definiendo roles, objetivos y metodologías de trabajo.
Trabajar en estrecha colaboración con los equipos de New Business, Customer Success, Marketing y Producto para alinear la estrategia de crecimiento con toda la organización.
Desarrollar un entendimiento profundo del ecosistema de colegios privados en México: perfiles de los centros de alta exigencia académica, procesos de decisión, competencia y estacionalidad.
Representar las necesidades y oportunidades del mercado mexicano ante el equipo global de Innovamat para influir en la hoja de ruta de producto.
Trabajar con RevOps en la construcción y gestión del pipeline en Salesforce, garantizando calidad del dato y precisión en el forecasting.
Colaborar con el equipo global de Growth para trasladar y adaptar al contexto mexicano las best practices y experimentos que funcionan en otros mercados.
Experiencia: 2 o más años en roles de Growth, Desarrollo de Negocio o Ventas B2B en entornos SaaS, EdTech o startups/scaleups de alto crecimiento.
Mentalidad data-driven: capacidad para definir métricas, diseñar experimentos, interpretar resultados y escalar lo que funciona con rigor analítico.
Visión comercial estratégica: habilidad para identificar oportunidades de mercado, desarrollar alianzas y construir canales de adquisición escalables.
Liderazgo de equipos: experiencia construyendo y desarrollando equipos en entornos de alta ambigüedad y crecimiento acelerado.
Conocimiento del segmento de colegios privados de alto nivel en México (redes, agrupaciones, procesos de decisión) — muy valorado, no excluyente.
Idiomas: inglés fluido (imprescindible para la coordinación con el equipo global).
Propósito: interés y pasión genuina por transformar la educación y la enseñanza de las matemáticas en México.
Un rol de construcción: la oportunidad de ser el/la arquitecto/a del crecimiento de Innovamat en México, con autonomía real para definir la estrategia, construir el equipo y ejecutarla.
Impacto directo y medible: cada nuevo colegio incorporado representa cientos de niños y niñas aprendiendo matemáticas de manera diferente.
Equipo y respaldo global: acceso al conocimiento, herramientas y experiencia de un equipo global líder en EdTech, presente en más de 10 países.
Línea de carrera en LATAM: posibilidad de liderar el crecimiento regional conforme Innovamat escala en América Latina.
Cultura Innovamat: ambiente horizontal, dinámico y de alto rendimiento, donde se premia la iniciativa, la autonomía y el debate constructivo.
Flexibilidad: esquema de trabajo híbrido desde Ciudad de México, con balance saludable entre vida profesional y personal.
description:
About the job

🚀 Sobre el rol

El/la Growth & Strategy Specialist de México es el/la máxima responsable de diseñar y ejecutar la estrategia de crecimiento de Innovamat en el país. En un mercado con alto potencial y foco en los mejores colegios privados de México, su misión es construir un motor de adquisición de alto rendimiento que acelere el crecimiento de nuestra base de clientes y consolide a Innovamat como la referencia en educación matemática de los centros de excelencia en el país.

Es un rol que combina visión estratégica, mentalidad experimental y ejecución comercial de alto impacto. La persona en este rol tendrá que construir desde la estrategia hasta el equipo, con autonomia real para hacerlo.

🌎 Puntos clave

Estrategia de crecimiento


Diseñar y liderar la estrategia de go-to-market para México: segmentación del mercado de colegios privados, priorización geográfica y definición de canales de adquisición.
Definir y ser owner de las métricas clave: nuevos colegios adquiridos, valor de pipeline, CAC, tasa de conversión y penetración en el segmento premium del mercado.
Construir relaciones y alianzas con redes de colegios privados, asociaciones educativas e instituciones clave para acelerar la adquisición.

Optimizar el funnel de adquisición de extremo a extremo, desde el primer contacto hasta el cierre y el handoff a Customer Success.

Trabajar en estrecha colaboración con los equipos de New Business, Customer Success, Marketing y Producto para alinear la estrategia de crecimiento con toda la organización.

Desarrollar un entendimiento profundo del ecosistema de colegios privados en México: perfiles de los centros de alta exigencia académica, procesos de decisión, competencia y estacionalidad.
Representar las necesidades y oportunidades del mercado mexicano ante el equipo global de Innovamat para influir en la hoja de ruta de producto.

Colaborar con el equipo global de Growth para trasladar y adaptar al contexto mexicano las best practices y experimentos que funcionan en otros mercados.


🔎 Qué buscamos

Mentalidad data-driven: capacidad para definir métricas, diseñar experimentos, interpretar resultados y escalar lo que funciona con rigor analítico.

Conocimiento del segmento de colegios privados de alto nivel en México (redes, agrupaciones, procesos de decisión) — muy valorado, no excluyente.

Propósito: interés y pasión genuina por transformar la educación y la enseñanza de las matemáticas en México.


Qué ofrecemos


Un rol de construcción: la oportunidad de ser el/la arquitecto/a del crecimiento de Innovamat en México, con autonomía real para definir la estrategia, construir el equipo y ejecutarla.
Impacto directo y medible: cada nuevo colegio incorporado representa cientos de niños y niñas aprendiendo matemáticas de manera diferente.

title: Perfomance Marketing Specialist
skills: Ownership - We own our successes & our failures as a team.
Excellence - We do everything to the best of our ability & always seek to achieve a new level of excellence in our work.
Inclusion - We believe we are stronger together and actively work to promote a safe, diverse, inclusive, and respectful culture.
Customer Obsession - We understand the value Klar can bring to its customers & it’s always at the forefront of our decisions.
Klarity - We communicate clearly & with authenticity. It’s in our name & it’s what we do.
Reporting: Create weekly and ad-hoc performance reports. Make financial analysis of campaign spend.
Own campaign tracking, attribution setup, and pixel/event health.
Operate and optimize paid campaigns: Constant monitoring and daily adjustments of paid ads campaigns on Google, Facebook and new channels.
Launch new campaigns, ad sets, creatives, and targeting strategies
Monitor performance daily and implement data-driven optimizations.
Run A/B tests on creatives, audiences, landing pages, and funnels.
Collaborate with creative and product teams to improve conversion.
KEY KPIs: New users and approved credit lines, Cost per Acquisition (CpA), and ROAS.
Bachelor's degree in Industrial Engineering, Business Engineering, Digital Transformation Engineering, (or similar) with an orientation to data analysis.
2+ years hands-on experience running performance campaigns on: Meta and Google.
Strong analytical skills, comfort working with performance dashboards and experience with SQL.
Experience with conversion tracking, pixels, and attribution tools.
Ability to do Data-driven decision-making.
High ownership, attention to detail, and execution speed.
Advanced English level.
Experience in fintech, apps, or subscription products is a plus.
Competitive salary based on performance and experience
Chance of earning Klar stock options
15 days of paid vacation per year; plus extended maternity and paternity leaves
Vacation premium
Food vouchers
30 days of Christmas bonus
Medical Insurance
Computer devices
Well hub subscription to offer mental and physical health
Sponsored coaching and therapy sessions
A modern centrally located office in Mexico City with free drinks, snacks, and regular social events
International work environment with amazing and highly skilled people
A world-class team that helps you evolve your skills in areas you're interested in
description:
About the job

About Klar

Let’s start with the basics! Klar is a Mexican fintech whose mission is to democratize and revolutionize how financial services are delivered in Mexico — especially since half of the population still doesn’t have a bank account. Since our launch in 2019, we’ve served more than 7 million users with a growing suite of debit and credit products, and the future is brighter than ever.

Today, we’re not only scaling our consumer business, but also building Klar Empresas (B2B) from the ground up — a new division focused on empowering Mexican SMEs with the financial tools, capital, and services they need to grow. This is an exciting new chapter where we get to design and build from scratch while leveraging the trust and scale of Klar’s platform.

We are also proud to have been certified as a “Great Place to Work” in Mexico — something we’ve worked hard to achieve and will continue to build on.

Our people

If you join us at Klar, you’ll be welcomed to a team which is rich in many talents and we are very proud! With our head office in Mexico City, and remote tech hubs in Berlin and Argentina, we are always learning something new about another culture or language. With so many people from different backgrounds and walks of life (young professionals, parents, LGBTQ+, neurodivergence), you’ll definitely find your people here!

Our values


Ownership - We own our successes & our failures as a team.
Excellence - We do everything to the best of our ability & always seek to achieve a new level of excellence in our work.
Inclusion - We believe we are stronger together and actively work to promote a safe, diverse, inclusive, and respectful culture.
Customer Obsession - We understand the value Klar can bring to its customers & it’s always at the forefront of our decisions.
Klarity - We communicate clearly & with authenticity. It’s in our name & it’s what we do.


The position and your daily adventures

We’re looking for a Performance Marketing Specialist to execute, optimize, and scale paid acquisition campaigns across Google, Meta, and other performance platforms. This is a hands-on role for someone who loves data, experimentation, and turning budgets into measurable growth.

You will own the day-to-day operation of performance campaigns, continuously improving CAC, ROAS, and funnel conversion, working closely with creative, analytics, and growth teams. You’ll be part of a high-performance team managing a US$2M+ monthly budget.

What you can expect:


Reporting: Create weekly and ad-hoc performance reports. Make financial analysis of campaign spend.
Own campaign tracking, attribution setup, and pixel/event health.
Operate and optimize paid campaigns: Constant monitoring and daily adjustments of paid ads campaigns on Google, Facebook and new channels.
Launch new campaigns, ad sets, creatives, and targeting strategies
Monitor performance daily and implement data-driven optimizations.
Run A/B tests on creatives, audiences, landing pages, and funnels.
Collaborate with creative and product teams to improve conversion.
KEY KPIs: New users and approved credit lines, Cost per Acquisition (CpA), and ROAS. 


What we are looking for:


Bachelor's degree in Industrial Engineering, Business Engineering, Digital Transformation Engineering, (or similar) with an orientation to data analysis.
2+ years hands-on experience running performance campaigns on: Meta and Google.
Strong analytical skills, comfort working with performance dashboards and experience with SQL.
Experience with conversion tracking, pixels, and attribution tools.
Ability to do Data-driven decision-making.
High ownership, attention to detail, and execution speed.
Advanced English level.
Experience in fintech, apps, or subscription products is a plus.


Our offer to you:


Competitive salary based on performance and experience
Chance of earning Klar stock options
15 days of paid vacation per year; plus extended maternity and paternity leaves
Vacation premium
Food vouchers
30 days of Christmas bonus
Medical Insurance
Computer devices
Well hub subscription to offer mental and physical health 
Sponsored coaching and therapy sessions 
A modern centrally located office in Mexico City with free drinks, snacks, and regular social events
International work environment with amazing and highly skilled people
A world-class team that helps you evolve your skills in areas you're interested in


Klar is a safe place for everyone!

We trust our highly skilled and diverse team, and we’re committed to creating a welcoming and inclusive environment for new talents to flourish. We value diversity and welcome all applications regardless of gender, nationality, ethnic and social origin, religion/belief, physical abilities, age, sexual orientation and identity.

Should you require any accommodations through the recruitment process, please don’t hesitate to let us know how we can help!

description:
About the job

NOTE: INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR POSITION







Company Overview:

Sagan is an exclusive membership community for top executives, founders, and CEOs seeking to hire and maximize the impact of international talent. We bridge the gap between global talent and US-based businesses, connecting candidates from vibrant regions like Latin America, the Philippines, India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, and Africa with leading American companies. Sagan provides a high-performance remote work environment, ensuring access to world-class opportunities for top-tier professionals.




About the Company:

Sagan represents a globally recognized sales training and consulting organization led by one of the world's most influential sales trainers. The company is entering a new phase of growth focused on AI-powered sales enablement, training, education, and productivity solutions. Their mission is to help sales professionals and organizations adopt AI in practical, revenue-generating ways while expanding the reach and impact of their brand worldwide.




Position Overview:

We are seeking an experienced AI Growth & Demand Generation Lead to build and scale the company's visibility, demand generation, and revenue growth initiatives. This role goes far beyond traditional marketing management. The ideal candidate will combine growth marketing, brand building, demand generation, content strategy, AI-powered workflows, and revenue-focused execution to create a scalable marketing engine.




You will work directly with leadership to increase brand awareness, generate qualified opportunities, support product launches, and build repeatable systems that convert expertise into revenue.

Qualifications:

5+ years of experience in growth marketing, demand generation, digital marketing, or brand strategy.

Strong understanding of marketing funnels, customer journeys, and lead generation systems.

description:
About the job

BAT is evolving at pace into a global multi-category business. Our purpose is to create A Better Tomorrow™ by Building a Smokeless World. 

To achieve our ambition, we are looking for colleagues who are ready to join us on this journey! Tomorrow can’t wait, let’s shape it together! 

British American Tobacco México has an exciting opportunity for a Marketing Global Graduate in Monterrey

Role Summary

Our Global Graduate Program (GGP) is an entry-level opportunity designed for recent college graduates with up to 3 years of work experience who wish to fast track their career within a global organization, and have the possibility, after 18 months of successful completion of our program, to work as a Junior Manager in our organization. BAT is a market leader in over 55 countries with over 55,000 employees.

¿WHAT DOES THIS MEAN FOR YOU? - AN EXPERIENCE LIKE NO OTHER 

For the driven, passionate, and ambitious recent graduates, our Global Graduate Program is the first step towards bigger and better things. It’s a real job from day one with world-class training, international opportunities, and the chance to reach your full potential.

Become a Global Graduate in Marketing

Our Global Graduate Program aims to develop exceptional commercial & strategical business skills - fully understanding the dynamics of the market you are operating in, and how the BAT Marketing departments such as Brands, Trade Marketing & Development, Consumer Experience, Consumer Insights and Foresights, Customer Management work together to achieve outstanding business results.

You will work as part of our accelerated management program in different marketing rotations, starting your career journey at Trade Marketing & Distribution across Mexico and moving forward into different marketing subfunctions in Area Office Monterrey. These rotations will allow you to develop and present strategic business projects addressing real opportunities and creating action plans

Your Key Responsibilities Will Include


Act as the main point of contact for customers, managing orders, providing portfolio recommendations, and optimizing the overall sales process. 
Analyze market trends (Nielsen, Sell Out, Key Accounts) to generate insights and drive portfolio growth actions. 
Develop, monitor, and execute strategic projects for key clients, ensuring alignment with business objectives. 
Track account performance, KPIs, and market performance indicators, defining and implementing action plans. 
Manage demand planning, including NPI forecasts, cycle volumes, and ensuring forecast accuracy for new SKUs. 
Ensure product availability by monitoring inventory levels and coordinating supply chain and volume negotiations. 
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (TM&D, Brands, Marketing) to translate insights into execution plans and track results. 
Execute commercial and digital strategies, including trade engagement, e-commerce content, and support for multi-channel campaigns (social media, CRM, Paid Media, etc)


¿ What are we looking for? 


Bachelor´s degree in Finance, Finance and Accounting, Business Intelligence, Global Business, Strategy and Business Transformation, Data Science and Mathematics, Industrial and Systems Engineering, Digital Transformation in Business, Business Management and Administration, Economics, International Finance, Business Engineering Management, Marketing, or related field.
Recent graduates (graduation from 2024 to June 2026).
Up to 1 year of professional experience. Experience in Data Driven Projects.
Fluent in English (advanced proficiency in oral & writing).
Efficient use of MS Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, SharePoint, Teams).
Open to relocate globally - global mobility and national mobility.
Results-driven professional,data-driven, analytical and ambitious, with strong interpersonal, teamwork, and negotiation skills; proactive, adaptable, and self-motivated with the ability to work independently.
Have leadership skills demonstrated through either relevant professional experience or extra-curricular activities within prestigious companies, NGOs, student associations, etc.


¿What we offer you?


We offer a market leading annual performance bonus (subject to eligibility) 
Our range of benefits varies by country and includes diverse health plans, initiatives for work-life balance, transportation support, and a flexible holiday plan with additional incentives 
Your journey with us isn't limited by boundaries; it's propelled by your aspirations. Join us at BAT and become a part of an environment that thrives on internal advancement, where your career progression isn't just a statement – it's a reality we're eager to build together. Seize the opportunity and own your development; your next chapter starts here. 
You'll have access to online learning platforms and personalized growth programs to nurture your leadership skills 
We prioritize continuous improvement within a transformative environment, preparing for ongoing changes. 


¿WHY JOIN BAT?

We’re one of the few companies named as a Global Top Employer by the Top Employers Institute – certified in offering excellent employee conditions.

Collaboration, inclusion and partnership underpin everything we do here at BAT. We are looking forward to enabling every individual to thrive, regardless of gender, sexual orientation, marital or civil partnership status, gender reassignment, race, religion or belief, colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, skills, experience, education, socio-economic and professional background, veteran status, perspectives and thinking styles. We know that embracing talent from all backgrounds is what makes us stronger and best prepared to meet our business goals.

We see the career breaks as opportunities not obstacles. Through The Global Returners program, we support professionals looking to restart their careers after an extended absence from the workforce (e.g. time out caring for family, parental leave, national service, sabbatical and/or starting an own venture).

Come bring your difference and see what is possible for you at BAT. Learn more about our culture and our award-winning employee experience here.

If you require any reasonable adjustments or accommodations to help you perform at your best during the recruitment process, you are encouraged to notify us. We are fully committed to support you by making appropriate arrangements for you to demonstrate your full potential.

skills: Own day-to-day operations of our Google product advertising program (Performance Max, Shopping, Search Ads & more), managing campaigns against revenue ratio and efficiency targets globally.

Over time, extend ownership to additional emerging channels as the portfolio evolves, managing a tiered channel strategy with structured experiments and clear kill criteria.
Own the health and growth of the product catalog that powers all advertising: eligibility rules, feed quality, Google Merchant Center policies, and disapproval resolution.
Monitor catalog coverage and identify scaling levers, including eligibility filter tuning, MCA suspension reduction, and feed enrichment opportunities.
Partner with product and engineering teams on catalog pipeline improvements and product vetting automation.
Partner with engineering to maintain and improve conversion signal pipelines, ensuring signal quality, match rates, and attribution accuracy.
Work with data science on incrementality measurement, attribution model calibration, and valuation frameworks that inform spend allocation decisions.
Monitor signal health (click ID coverage, event volumes, platform-reported vs. internal attribution gaps) and escalate anomalies.
Work with creative strategy to develop effective product advertising programs across the funnel.
Actively communicate program performance, financial health, and strategic trade-offs to marketing leadership and finance stakeholders.
A successful track record managing SEM and product-feed-based advertising campaigns at scale (Shopping, Performance Max, or equivalent), with demonstrated ability to hit efficiency targets.
Direct experience with product feed management and merchant center operations: feed diagnostics, disapproval resolution, eligibility optimization, and catalog scaling.
Experience with SQL. You may not be able to write complex queries from scratch, but you have a high-level understanding of databases and how to query them.
Working knowledge of conversion tracking infrastructure: server-to-server signals, click ID mechanics, attribution windows, and how signal quality affects platform bidding.
Experience partnering with engineering and data science teams on technical marketing problems (feed pipelines, attribution, measurement frameworks).
Ability to design structured experiments with clear hypotheses, kill criteria, and measurement plans.
Strong stakeholder communication skills: you can translate technical performance data into clear narratives for marketing leadership and finance.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and can navigate through ambiguity and work with minimal guidance.
Care deeply about what you do and about making commerce better for everyone
Excel by seeking professional and personal hypergrowth
Keep up with an unrelenting pace (the week, not the quarter)
Be resilient and resourceful in face of ambiguity and thrive on (rather than endure) change
Bring critical thought and opinion
Put AI agents and tools to work on the tasks they're built for, and focus on the work only humans can do
Embrace differences and disagreement to get shit done and move forward
Work digital-first for your daily work
description:
About the job

About The Role

We are seeking an experienced, technically sharp marketing lead for Shopify's 3P Shop Campaigns program. You will own performance, experimentation, and scaling of our Google product advertising portfolio (Performance Max, Shopping, Search Ads), with the opportunity to expand into additional emerging channels over time.

This is not a typical paid media role. You will operate at the intersection of marketing, product feeds, and engineering. A significant part of your work involves managing campaigns with aggressive ROAS targets and expanding the product catalog that powers our ads: millions of merchant products flowing through feed pipelines, eligibility filters, and Google review processes. You will partner closely with engineering and data science teams on catalog health, conversion signal infrastructure, and measurement rigor.

The right person combines hands-on feed/shopping campaign experience with the strategic ability to manage a channel portfolio against efficiency targets, design high-velocity experiments, and communicate performance and trade-offs clearly to leadership.

Why Join Us

You’ll be part of a collaborative Growth team that values creativity, experimentation, and impact. With access to significant budgets, a strong data-driven culture, and exposure to cross-functional experts, you’ll have the opportunity to accelerate your performance media craft, while directly contributing to scaling our business.

Key Responsibilities

Portfolio & Campaign Management


Own day-to-day operations of our Google product advertising program (Performance Max, Shopping, Search Ads & more), managing campaigns against revenue ratio and efficiency targets globally.
Design and execute high-velocity testing pipelines across bidding strategies, campaign structures, audiences, creative, and landing pages.
Over time, extend ownership to additional emerging channels as the portfolio evolves, managing a tiered channel strategy with structured experiments and clear kill criteria.



Catalog & Feed Management


Own the health and growth of the product catalog that powers all advertising: eligibility rules, feed quality, Google Merchant Center policies, and disapproval resolution.
Monitor catalog coverage and identify scaling levers, including eligibility filter tuning, MCA suspension reduction, and feed enrichment opportunities.
Partner with product and engineering teams on catalog pipeline improvements and product vetting automation.



Conversion Signals & Measurement


Partner with engineering to maintain and improve conversion signal pipelines, ensuring signal quality, match rates, and attribution accuracy.
Work with data science on incrementality measurement, attribution model calibration, and valuation frameworks that inform spend allocation decisions.
Monitor signal health (click ID coverage, event volumes, platform-reported vs. internal attribution gaps) and escalate anomalies.



Cross-Functional & Communication


Work with creative strategy to develop effective product advertising programs across the funnel.
Actively communicate program performance, financial health, and strategic trade-offs to marketing leadership and finance stakeholders.



Qualifications


A successful track record managing SEM and product-feed-based advertising campaigns at scale (Shopping, Performance Max, or equivalent), with demonstrated ability to hit efficiency targets.
Direct experience with product feed management and merchant center operations: feed diagnostics, disapproval resolution, eligibility optimization, and catalog scaling.
Experience with SQL. You may not be able to write complex queries from scratch, but you have a high-level understanding of databases and how to query them.
Working knowledge of conversion tracking infrastructure: server-to-server signals, click ID mechanics, attribution windows, and how signal quality affects platform bidding.
Experience partnering with engineering and data science teams on technical marketing problems (feed pipelines, attribution, measurement frameworks).
Ability to design structured experiments with clear hypotheses, kill criteria, and measurement plans.
Strong stakeholder communication skills: you can translate technical performance data into clear narratives for marketing leadership and finance.
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and can navigate through ambiguity and work with minimal guidance.



About Shopify

Opportunity is not evenly distributed. Shopify puts independence within reach for anyone with a dream to start a business. We propel entrepreneurs and enterprises to scale the heights of their potential. Since 2006, we’ve grown to over 8,300 employees and generated over $1 trillion in sales for millions of merchants in 175 countries.

This is life-defining work that directly impacts people’s lives as much as it transforms your own. This is putting the power of the few in the hands of the many, is a future with more voices rather than fewer, and is creating more choices instead of an elite option.

About You

Moving at our pace brings a lot of change, complexity, and ambiguity—and a little bit of chaos. Shopifolk thrive on that and are comfortable being uncomfortable. That means Shopify is not the right place for everyone.

Before you apply, consider if you can:


Care deeply about what you do and about making commerce better for everyone
Excel by seeking professional and personal hypergrowth
Keep up with an unrelenting pace (the week, not the quarter)
Be resilient and resourceful in face of ambiguity and thrive on (rather than endure) change
Bring critical thought and opinion
Put AI agents and tools to work on the tasks they're built for, and focus on the work only humans can do
Embrace differences and disagreement to get shit done and move forward
Work digital-first for your daily work



We may use AI-enabled tools to screen, select, and assess applications. All AI outputs are reviewed and validated by our recruitment team.

Knowledge of Canva and Capcut
Strong communication skills to interact with clients of all backgrounds and educational levels.
Flexible working hours required in case of urgency or business need.
Positive, proactive approach and problem-solving attitude.



Our Offer And Benefits


Salary: up to 600 USD / monthly 
Location: Latam (Full Remote) 
Working hours: Full-time 40h/week
Contract: Freelance contract
Start Time: ASAP



WHY YOU MIGHT *NOT* BE EXCITED ABOUT US


We're a small team, so if you like more established companies, this role isn’t for you. You'll help create our company's culture.
We're an fast growing scaleup. Projects and priorities are likely to shift. You see this as an opportunity, not a problem.
You should be comfortable with navigating uncertainty.
WeLearn is a pro-startup, pro-tech organization. If that’s not your thing, this isn’t the place for you.



If you got this far down the list, you must be interested. While you may not possess all of the experience and skills we are looking for, passion for the role and a strong motivation go a long way. And…you have a thirst for learning, you are ambitious, and you like working with a team who shares those attributes.

About Us

At WeLearn we believe the human capital is the best asset of a company, hence you will interview directly with the Founders to whom you will be able to ask any question and who will tell you more about the project, their vision and your potential career path. If you love social media, you like to be surrounded by young, driven and like minded people and would like to start off your career in a tech startup with unlimited potential of growth, you are in the right place and we couldn’t be more excited to have you in our team.

WeLearn is a recently created startup, founded by two former Google and Facebook Strategist and Project Manager experiencing extremely rapid growth. At WeLearn we partner with influencers and content creators and we support them in running their businesses.

title: Search Senior Executive, GOC

description:
About the job

About WPP Media

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. With exceptional talent, trusted data and intelligence, and world-class partnerships – all united by our pioneering agentic marketing platform, WPP Open – we help clients navigate change, capture opportunity, and deliver transformational growth. 

WPP Media is WPP's AI-driven media operating unit, bringing together media, data, and partnerships to deliver creative personalisation at scale. Connected through WPP Open and powered by Open Intelligence, clients see exactly where, how, and why their media investment is working.

For more information, visit wppmedia.com.

Senior Executive Search, GOC

About WPP Media

WPP is the creative transformation company. We use the power of creativity to build better futures for our people, planet, clients and communities. For more information, visit wpp.com.

WPP Media is WPP’s global media collective. In a world where media is everywhere and in everything, we bring the best platform, people, and partners together to create limitless opportunities for growth. For more information, visit wppmedia.com

At WPP Media, We Believe In The Power Of Our Culture And Our People. It’s What Elevates Us To Deliver Exceptional Experiences For Both Our Clients And Each Other. In This Role It Will Be Critical To Embrace WPP & WPP Media’s Shared Core Values:


Be Extraordinary by Leading Collectively to Inspire transformational Creativity. 
Create an Open environment by Balancing People and Client Experiences by Cultivating Trust. 
Lead Optimistically by Championing Growth and Development to Mobilize the Enterprise. 


Role Summary And Impact

As part of the Global Operations Centre (GOC), you will work closely with teams across the United States, Europe, and Asia to deliver high-impact solutions in a collaborative, multicultural environment. We are looking for a Senior Executive Search who will:


Develop strong relationships with counterparts in WPP Media’s regional teams (Europe, United States and Asia). 
Support the implementation of digital campaigns and ads according to guidelines with platforms such as Google Ads, Bing Ads, Amazon Search, Search Ads 360, etc. 
Ensure campaigns comply with WPP Media’s global policies and guidelines. 
Monitor campaigns daily on each of the platforms and ensure accuracy of data across platforms for campaign reporting. 
Generate daily campaign reports for reporting to regional Search teams. 


Skills And Experience

If this opportunity looks right for you, we are looking for candidates who have:


Bachelor’s degree. 
Advanced English level (B2–C2). 
Have 1.5-2 years of experience implementing paid Search campaigns on platforms such as Google Ads, Bing Ads, Amazon Search, etc. 
Advanced Excel skills. 


Life At WPP Media & Benefits

Our passion for shaping the next era of media includes investing in our employees to help them do their best work, and we’re just as committed to employee growth as we are to responsible media investment. WPP Media employees can tap into the global WPP Media & WPP networks to pursue their passions, grow their networks, and learn at the cutting edge of marketing and advertising. We have a variety of employee resource groups and host frequent in-office events showcasing team wins, sharing thought leadership, and celebrating holidays and milestone events. Our benefits include access to internal learning platforms, certifications, and training sessions, life insurance, wellness programs, significant paid time off, preferential partner discounts, employee mental health awareness day, and more. 

WPP Media is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

For Mexico:

This position is available in a Remote or Hybrid Work Modality. The candidate must perform their work from any location within Mexico. Only Mexican nationals residing in the country or foreign nationals with a valid work permit may apply. Remote work from abroad is not permitted under any circumstances.

For Colombia:

This position is in a Remote or Hybrid Work Modality and must be performed within Colombian territory. Only candidates residing in Colombia with a valid work permit may apply. Remote work contracts do not allow employment or relocation abroad.

Please read our Privacy Notice (https://www.wppmedia.com/pages/privacy-policy ) for more information on how we process the information you provide.

While we appreciate all applications received, only those candidates selected for an interview will be contacted. 

Please read our Privacy Notice for more information on how we process the information you provide.

title: CRO Manager
skills: Lead the development and execution of A/B tests, multivariate tests, and personalization strategies to improve conversion rates and optimize user experiences.
Conduct and analyze user research, heatmaps, session recordings, and surveys to identify drop-off points and pain points in the user journey.
Develop and implement a comprehensive CRO strategy focused on increasing conversions across all digital platforms, including web, mobile, and app interfaces.
Work closely with UX/UI designers, developers, and content teams to create and execute on optimization plans. Ensure changes are effectively implemented and tracked.
Use tools such as Google Analytics, Hotjar, and other CRO platforms to measure, analyze, and report on the effectiveness of experiments.
Apply a user-centered approach to solving design and usability challenges, creating smooth and effective user flows that drive desired outcomes.
Continuously refine the CRO process, advocating for best practices in testing and optimization within the organization.
Work closely with stakeholders from marketing, sales, and product teams in an agile environment, ensuring alignment on goals and strategies.
Minimum 3-4 years of experience in a CRO or digital optimization role, with a proven track record of improving conversion rates.
Strong understanding of A/B testing, multivariate testing, and personalization techniques. Expertise in using testing platforms (e.g., Optimizely, VWO) and analytical tools like Google Analytics.
Ability to draw actionable insights from user behavior data, and translate these insights into optimization plans.
Comfortable with the technical aspects of web analytics, basic HTML/CSS, and JavaScript, with the ability to communicate requirements to developers.
Understanding of UX/UI principles and how design impacts conversion rates.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present insights and recommendations to cross-functional teams.
High Ownership: Lead growth across a $100M+ brand platform
Creative Autonomy: Big sandbox, bold ideas encouraged
Cross-Brand Impact: Learn and scale across multiple industries and audiences
Fast Execution: We move quickly and prioritize performance
description:
About the job

About Pathfinder

Pathfinder is a brand platform on a mission to acquire, scale, and revitalize exceptional consumer brands in the U.S. Backed by a committed capital vehicle and led by founders with experience spanning Stanford GSB, Harvard Law, Y Combinator, Forbes 30U30, and deep expertise in e-commerce, marketing, and finance, we acquire high-potential DTC brands and unlock growth through hands-on operational excellence. We have acquired several brands to-date and are building a dream team of experts who want to turn previously mismanaged brands into breakout successes.

Our long-term vision: build a $100M+ portfolio of beloved, performance-driven consumer brands.




About the Role

We’re hiring an exceptional CRO Manager to turn traffic into purchases for 2 in-house DTC brands.

In this remote role, you will own and execute conversion optimization strategies to maximize website performance and marketing funnel efficiency. 




You’ll work closely with the Head of Growth, Design, and web development teams to create data-driven experiments, implement UX/UI improvements, and drive measurable conversion rate growth. 




What You’ll Do: 

Lead the development and execution of A/B tests, multivariate tests, and personalization strategies to improve conversion rates and optimize user experiences.
Conduct and analyze user research, heatmaps, session recordings, and surveys to identify drop-off points and pain points in the user journey.
Develop and implement a comprehensive CRO strategy focused on increasing conversions across all digital platforms, including web, mobile, and app interfaces.
Work closely with UX/UI designers, developers, and content teams to create and execute on optimization plans. Ensure changes are effectively implemented and tracked.
Use tools such as Google Analytics, Hotjar, and other CRO platforms to measure, analyze, and report on the effectiveness of experiments.
Apply a user-centered approach to solving design and usability challenges, creating smooth and effective user flows that drive desired outcomes.
Continuously refine the CRO process, advocating for best practices in testing and optimization within the organization.
Work closely with stakeholders from marketing, sales, and product teams in an agile environment, ensuring alignment on goals and strategies.




Skills, Knowledge, and Experience:
Minimum 3-4 years of experience in a CRO or digital optimization role, with a proven track record of improving conversion rates.
Strong understanding of A/B testing, multivariate testing, and personalization techniques. Expertise in using testing platforms (e.g., Optimizely, VWO) and analytical tools like Google Analytics.
Ability to draw actionable insights from user behavior data, and translate these insights into optimization plans.
Comfortable with the technical aspects of web analytics, basic HTML/CSS, and JavaScript, with the ability to communicate requirements to developers.
Understanding of UX/UI principles and how design impacts conversion rates.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to present insights and recommendations to cross-functional teams.




Why Join Pathfinder?

High Ownership: Lead growth across a $100M+ brand platform
Creative Autonomy: Big sandbox, bold ideas encouraged
Cross-Brand Impact: Learn and scale across multiple industries and audiences
Fast Execution: We move quickly and prioritize performance

title: E-commerce Simulation Expert (Remote)
skills: Role: E-commerce Simulation Expert (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: $40 - $50/hour
description:
About the job

Role: E-commerce Simulation Expert (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Payout: $40 - $50/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking an E-commerce Simulation Expert to work on a contract basis. The role focuses on building and refining synthetic e-commerce environments that train and evaluate advanced AI models. Your expertise in real-world commerce will directly shape how AI systems learn, reason, and perform across critical domains. This work supports the development of reliable AI agents and contributes to the evolution of human-in-the-loop AI training.




Key Responsibilities:

• Design and construct synthetic digital stores with realistic product assortments, logical category structures, and authentic pricing models to reflect real-world e-commerce complexity.

• Generate, structure, and maintain comprehensive product catalogs ensuring high data quality, logical consistency, and alignment with market standards.

• Develop diverse shopping scenarios and user prompts that simulate varied consumer intents, preferences, and challenging edge cases to stress-test AI behavior.

• Create evaluation rubrics and conduct rigorous quality assurance to ensure generated data and simulations meet domain-specific accuracy and realism requirements.

• Collaborate with AI teams to refine prompt engineering and scenario design for continuous improvement of AI training datasets.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Demonstrated experience in e-commerce operations, including product data management, catalog structuring, or retail systems with a focus on data integrity and real-world relevance.

• Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills to design complex, logically consistent product hierarchies and pricing logic.

• Familiarity with AI/LLM prompting and evaluation principles, or ability to quickly learn how to structure inputs that generate high-quality training data for AI systems.

• Background in scenario design and user behavior modeling, preferably in retail, consumer goods, or related domains.

• Attention to detail and a quality assurance mindset to maintain high standards in synthetic data generation and simulation environments.

• Self-directed work style with ability to manage multiple asynchronous tasks and deliver consistent, high-quality output under defined deadlines.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers the chance to contribute directly to the development of frontier AI systems by leveraging real-world e-commerce expertise. You will work within a global network supporting AI research, with exposure to cutting-edge reinforcement learning environments and agent-based AI training. The position is ideal for professionals seeking meaningful impact through applied domain knowledge in a rapidly evolving field.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.




Apply Now!

description:
About the job

Sobre nosotros

En Koltin, estamos rediseñando la forma en que cuidamos a nuestros papás, mamás, abuelos y abuelas, con el objetivo de ayudarles a mantenerse saludables, activos e independientes durante el mayor tiempo posible. Lo hacemos a través de nuestras membresías de salud, que integran:

Atención clínica enfocada en la prevención
Cobertura médica de gastos mayores
Programas de bienestar y comunidad

Somos la primera empresa en México que protege a personas adultas mayores, desde los 50 hasta los 84 años. Estamos creciendo rápidamente en todo el país, con un propósito claro: transformar la experiencia de salud para las Grandes Personas, haciéndola más humana, accesible y digna.




Sobre el Puesto

Koltin tiene miles de leads que ya conocen el producto, mostraron interés y no compraron. Ese backlog es el activo más eficiente del negocio — el costo de adquisición ya fue pagado. Hoy está subutilizado. Tu trabajo es convertirlo en ventas reales con un sistema data-driven.

Tu meta en septiembre: 860 leads del backlog convertidos en venta — el 56% del total de ventas de Koltin. El rol es 100% data-driven: cada decisión se toma con números. Si construís el sistema y lo hacés escalar, el rol crece con vos.

description:
About the job

About Charly Agency

 Charly Agency is a growing digital agency specializing in web design, UX/UI, branding, ecommerce, and development for clients primarily in the US. Operating globally with offices in San Francisco and Barcelona, we partner with brands ranging from startups to enterprises. Our team is spread across Europe and Latin America and we are scaling fast.




About the Role

 We are looking for a freelance copywriter to join our pool of creative collaborators. You will write across brand, web, and marketing, working closely with our design and strategy team to bring client projects to life through sharp, purposeful copy.

This is not a beginner role. We want someone who can take a brief, understand the business behind it, and produce copy that is clear, purposeful, and built to convert — without hand-holding. Mistakes happen, but high quality, great communication, and honesty are non-negotiable. No vague communication, no hiding behind "I was waiting for feedback."




What You'll Do

Write website copy across homepages, landing pages, product pages, and about pages
Develop brand voice guidelines and messaging frameworks
Write case studies, blog posts, and marketing content
Collaborate with designers to ensure copy and design work together




What We're Looking For

Strong portfolio of web and brand copywriting
Excellent written English, this is non-negotiable
Experience writing for DTC, SaaS, B2B, or CPG brands is a plus
Comfortable delivering quickly under tight deadlines without sacrificing quality
Clear and thoughtful approach to using AI in your workflow
Reliable, communicative, and comfortable working async
Eager to learn, open to direction, and quick on feedback
Agency experience is a strong plus




About Us

 Charly is a creative agency with a warm, international team that takes the work seriously without taking ourselves too seriously. We collaborate across time zones, support each other, and care a lot about doing great work for clients we genuinely like. If you are kind, talented, and ambitious, you will fit right in.




To Apply, send:

Your strongest copywriting samples (website, landing page, or brand copy preferred)
Examples of projects you wrote for and your role in them
Your availability and preferred working hours
Your expected rate (hourly or per project)

title: Performance Marketing Specialist
skills: Ownership - We own our successes & our failures as a team.
Excellence - We do everything to the best of our ability & always seek to achieve a new level of excellence in our work.
Inclusion - We believe we are stronger together and actively work to promote a safe, diverse, inclusive, and respectful culture.
Customer Obsession - We understand the value Klar can bring to its customers & it’s always at the forefront of our decisions.
Klarity - We communicate clearly & with authenticity. It’s in our name & it’s what we do.
